revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: ''''東大寺要録'''（とうだいじようろく）は、[[平安時代]]後期に作成された[[東大寺]]の寺誌。10巻10章。 == 概要 == 序文に...'
revision.text:
'''東大寺要録'''（とうだいじようろく）は、[[平安時代]]後期に作成された[[東大寺]]の寺誌。10巻10章。
== 概要 == 序文によれば、[[嘉承]]元年（[[1106年]]）に東大寺の僧が衰微した寺の再興を願って編纂したとあるが、編者名などは不明。その後、[[長承]]3年（[[1134年]]）になって[[観厳]]が増補・再編して今日知られる形態になったという。ただし、原本・観厳伝本ともに現存しておらず、観厳の伝本に更に補筆されたものの写本の一部が[[醍醐寺]]や東大寺に残されており、いずれも国の[[重要文化財]]とされている。
本願・縁起・供養・諸院・諸会・諸宗・別当・封戸水田・末寺・雑事の各章からなり、今日では散逸したものも含めた多くの史料・文書からの引用記事を含んでおり、創建当時から院政期にかけての[[東大寺の歴史]]を知る上で貴重な史料となっている。また、数多くの[[先例]]を記しているため、東大寺内部においては一種の規範的効果を有していた。
== 参考文献 == *永村眞「東大寺要録」（『国史大辞典 10』（吉川弘文館、1989年） ISBN 978-4-642-00510-4） *稲葉伸道「東大寺要録」（『日本史大事典 5』（平凡社、1993年） ISBN 978-4-642-00510-4） *久野修義「東大寺要録」（『日本歴史大事典 3』（小学館、2001年） ISBN 978-4-09-523003-0）
== 関連項目 == *[[東大寺続要録]] *[[東大寺別当次第]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:とうたいしようろく}} [[Category:仏教史書]] [[Category:東大寺]] [[Category:12世紀の書籍]]

'''東大寺要録'''（とうだいじようろく）は、[[平安時代]]後期に作成された[[東大寺]]の寺誌。10巻10章。
== 概要 == 序文によれば、[[嘉承]]元年（[[1106年]]）に東大寺の僧が衰微した寺の再興を願って編纂したとあるが、編者名などは不明。その後、[[長承]]3年（[[1134年]]）になって[[観厳]]が増補・再編して今日知られる形態になったという。ただし、原本・観厳伝本ともに現存しておらず、観厳の伝本に更に補筆されたものの写本の一部が[[醍醐寺]]や東大寺に残されており、いずれも国の[[重要文化財]]とされている。
本願・縁起・供養・諸院・諸会・諸宗・別当・封戸水田・末寺・雑事の各章からなり、今日では散逸したものも含めた多くの史料・文書からの引用記事を含んでおり、創建当時から院政期にかけての[[東大寺の歴史]]を知る上で貴重な史料となっている。また、数多くの[[先例]]を記しているため、東大寺内部においては一種の規範的効果を有していた。
[[国書刊行会]]から活字版が出版されている。
== 参考文献 == *永村眞「東大寺要録」（『国史大辞典 10』（吉川弘文館、1989年） ISBN 978-4-642-00510-4） *稲葉伸道「東大寺要録」（『日本史大事典 5』（平凡社、1993年） ISBN 978-4-642-00510-4） *久野修義「東大寺要録」（『日本歴史大事典 3』（小学館、2001年） ISBN 978-4-09-523003-0）
== 関連項目 == *[[東大寺続要録]] *[[東大寺別当次第]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:とうたいしようろく}} [[Category:仏教史書]] [[Category:東大寺]] [[Category:12世紀の書籍]]

'''東大寺要録'''（とうだいじようろく）は、[[平安時代]]後期に作成された[[東大寺]]の寺誌。10巻10章。
== 概要 == 序文によれば、[[嘉承]]元年（[[1106年]]）に東大寺の僧が衰微した寺の再興を願って編纂したとあるが、編者名などは不明。その後、[[長承]]3年（[[1134年]]）になって[[観厳]]が増補・再編して今日知られる形態になったという。元は巻子本。ただし、原本・観厳伝本ともに現存しておらず、観厳の伝本に更に補筆されたものの写本の一部が[[醍醐寺]]（[[仁治]]2年（[[1241年]]）奥書）や東大寺（[[文明 (日本)|文明]]17年（[[1485年]]）奥書）に残されており、いずれも国の[[重要文化財]]とされている。

'''東大寺要録'''（とうだいじようろく）は、[[平安時代]]後期に作成された[[東大寺]]の寺誌。10巻10章。
== 概要 == 序文によれば、[[嘉承]]元年（[[1106年]]）に東大寺の僧が衰微した寺の再興を願って編纂したとあるが、編者名などは不明。その後、[[長承]]3年（[[1134年]]）になって[[観厳]]が増補・再編して今日知られる形態になったという。元は巻子本。ただし、原本・観厳伝本ともに現存しておらず、観厳の伝本に更に補筆されたものの写本の一部が[[醍醐寺]]（[[仁治]]2年（[[1241年]]）奥書）や東大寺（[[文明 (日本)|文明]]17年（[[1485年]]）奥書）に残されており、いずれも国の[[重要文化財]]とされている。
本願・縁起・供養・諸院・諸会・諸宗・別当・封戸水田・末寺・雑事の各章からなり、今日では散逸したものも含めた多くの史料・文書からの引用記事を含んでおり、創建当時から院政期にかけての[[東大寺の歴史]]を知る上で貴重な史料となっている。また、数多くの[[先例]]を記しているため、東大寺内部においては一種の規範的効果を有していた。

revision.comment: Created page with "'''Tōdaiji Yōroku''' (東大寺要録) is a temple chronicle of {{ill|Tōdai-ji|ja|東大寺}}, compiled in the late {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}. It consists of 10 volumes divided into 10 chapters. == Overview == According to its preface, the chronicle was compiled in {{ill|Kashō|ja|嘉承}} 1 (1106) by a monk from Tōdaiji who wished to restore the declining temple. The editor's name remains unknown. Later, in {{ill|Chōshō|ja|長承}} 3 (1134), {{ill|Kang..."
revision.text:
'''Tōdaiji Yōroku''' (東大寺要録) is a temple chronicle of {{ill|Tōdai-ji|ja|東大寺}}, compiled in the late {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}. It consists of 10 volumes divided into 10 chapters.
== Overview == According to its preface, the chronicle was compiled in {{ill|Kashō|ja|嘉承}} 1 (1106) by a monk from Tōdaiji who wished to restore the declining temple. The editor's name remains unknown. Later, in {{ill|Chōshō|ja|長承}} 3 (1134), {{ill|Kangen|ja|観厳}} revised and supplemented the text, shaping it into the form known today. Originally a scroll manuscript, neither the original nor Kangen's version survives. However, portions of later handwritten copies, which incorporate Kangen's revisions and additional annotations, are preserved at {{ill|Daigo-ji|ja|醍醐寺}} (bearing a colophon dated {{ill|Ninji|ja|仁治}} 2 (1241)) and at Tōdaiji itself (with a colophon from {{ill|Bunmei|ja|文明}} 17 (1485)). Both copies are designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Properties of Japan|ja|重要文化財}}.
The chronicle comprises chapters on topics such as foundation prayers, origins, memorial services, sub-temples, rituals, sects, abbots, tax-exempt estates, subsidiary temples, and miscellaneous matters. It incorporates many excerpts from historical materials and documents, including sources that are now lost, making it an invaluable resource for understanding Tōdaiji's history from its founding through the Insei period. Additionally, its numerous precedents served as a kind of regulatory guide within the temple.
A printed version was published by {{ill|Kokusho Kankōkai|ja|国書刊行会}}.
== References == * Nagamura Makoto, "Tōdaiji Yōroku" in *{{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典}} Vol. 10* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1989) ISBN 978-4-642-00510-4. * {{ill|Inaba Nobumichi|ja|稲葉伸道}}, "Tōdaiji Yōroku" in *{{ill|Nihonshi Daijiten|ja|日本史大事典}} Vol. 5* (Heibonsha, 1993) ISBN 978-4-642-00510-4. * *{{ill|Hachiman Usagu Gotakusenshū|ja|八幡宇佐宮御託宣集}}*, edited by Shinun, 1313. * Hiraoka Sadami, "Tōdaiji Yōroku" in *{{ill|Heian Jidai-shi Jiten|ja|平安時代史事典}}* (Kadokawa Shoten, 1994) ISBN 978-4-04-031700-7. * Kuno Shūgi, "Tōdaiji Yōroku" in *{{ill|Nihon Rekishi Daijiten|ja|日本歴史大事典}} Vol. 3* (Shogakukan, 2001) ISBN 978-4-09-523003-0.
=== Related Literature === * *Tōdaiji Yōroku*, edited by Tsutsui Eishun, Kokusho Kankōkai, 1971, revised edition 2003. * *Tōdaiji Zoku (Continued) Yōroku*, supervised by Tsutsui Kanshū, edited by the same research team, Kokusho Kankōkai, 2013. * *Essays on the Ancient World of Tōdaiji: Revisiting "Tōdaiji Yōroku"*, Tōdaiji *The Great Buddha Symposium*, Vol. 14, Hōzōkan, 2017. * *Tōdaiji Yōroku Vol. 1: Tōdaiji Series*, edited by Tōdaiji Historical Research Institute, Hōzōkan, 2019. * *Tōdaiji Yōroku Vol. 2: Tōdaiji Series*, edited by Tōdaiji Historical Research Institute, Hōzōkan, 2020.
== See Also == * {{ill|Tōdaiji Zoku Yōroku|ja|東大寺続要録}} * {{ill|Tōdaiji Bettō Shidai|ja|東大寺別当次第}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:Todaiji Yōroku}} [[Category:Buddhist historical texts]] [[Category:Cultural properties of Tōdai-ji]] [[Category:12th-century books]] [[Category:Heian-period historical texts]] [[Category:Nara-period Buddhism]] [[Category:Heian-period Buddhism]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Nara City]]

'''Tōdaiji Yōroku''' (東大寺要録) is a temple chronicle of {{ill|Tōdai-ji|ja|東大寺}}, compiled in the late {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}. It consists of 10 volumes divided into 10 chapters.

The editor's name remains unknown. Later, in {{ill|Chōshō|ja|長承}} 3 (1134), {{ill|Kangen|ja|観厳}} revised and supplemented the text, shaping it into the form known today. Originally a scroll manuscript, neither the original nor Kangen's version survives. However, portions of later handwritten copies, which incorporate Kangen's revisions and additional annotations, are preserved at {{ill|Daigo-ji|ja|醍醐寺}} (bearing a colophon dated {{ill|Ninji|ja|仁治}} 2 (1241)) and at Tōdaiji itself (with a colophon from {{ill|Bunmei|ja|文明}} 17 (1485)). Both copies are designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Properties of Japan|ja|重要文化財}}.
The chronicle comprises chapters on topics such as foundation prayers, origins, memorial services, sub-temples, rituals, sects, abbots, tax-exempt estates, subsidiary temples, and miscellaneous matters. It incorporates many excerpts from historical materials and documents, including sources that are now lost, making it an invaluable resource for understanding Tōdaiji's history from its founding through the Insei period. Additionally, its numerous precedents served as a kind of regulatory guide within the temple.
A printed version was published by {{ill|Kokusho Kankōkai|ja|国書刊行会}}.

== Overview == According to its preface, the chronicle was compiled in {{ill|Kashō|ja|嘉承}} 1 (1106) by a monk from Tōdaiji who wished to restore the declining temple. The editor's name remains unknown. Later, in {{ill|Chōshō|ja|長承}} 3 (1134), {{ill|Kangen|ja|観厳}} revised and supplemented the text, shaping it into the form known today. Originally a scroll manuscript, neither the original nor Kangen's version survives. However, portions of later handwritten copies, which incorporate Kangen's revisions and additional annotations, are preserved at {{ill|Daigo-ji|ja|醍醐寺}} (bearing a colophon dated {{ill|Ninji|ja|仁治}} 2 (1241)) and at Tōdaiji itself (with a colophon from {{ill|Bunmei|ja|文明}} 17 (1485)). Both copies are designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Properties of Japan|ja|重要文化財}}.
The chronicle comprises chapters on topics such as foundation prayers, origins, memorial services, sub-temples, rituals, sects, abbots, tax-exempt estates, subsidiary temples, and miscellaneous matters. It incorporates many excerpts from historical materials and documents, including sources that are now lost, making it an invaluable resource for understanding Tōdaiji's history from its founding through the Insei period. Additionally, its numerous precedents served as a kind of regulatory guide within the temple.
A printed version was published by {{ill|Kokusho Kankōkai|ja|国書刊行会}}.

revision.text:
'''Tōdaiji Yōroku''' (東大寺要録) is a temple chronicle of {{ill|Tōdai-ji|ja|東大寺}}, compiled in the late {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}. It consists of 10 volumes divided into 10 chapters.
== Overview == According to its preface, the chronicle was compiled in {{ill|Kashō|ja|嘉承}} 1 (1106) by a monk from Tōdaiji who wished to restore the declining temple. The editor's name remains unknown. Later, in {{ill|Chōshō|ja|長承}} 3 (1134), {{ill|Kangen|ja|観厳}} revised and supplemented the text, shaping it into the form known today. Originally a scroll manuscript, neither the original nor Kangen's version survives. However, portions of later handwritten copies, which incorporate Kangen's revisions and additional annotations, are preserved at {{ill|Daigo-ji|ja|醍醐寺}} (bearing a colophon dated {{ill|Ninji|ja|仁治}} 2 (1241)) and at Tōdaiji itself (with a colophon from {{ill|Bunmei|ja|文明}} 17 (1485)). Both copies are designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Properties of Japan|ja|重要文化財}}.
The chronicle comprises chapters on topics such as foundation prayers, origins, memorial services, sub-temples, rituals, sects, abbots, tax-exempt estates, subsidiary temples, and miscellaneous matters. It incorporates many excerpts from historical materials and documents, including sources that are now lost, making it an invaluable resource for understanding Tōdaiji's history from its founding through the Insei period. Additionally, its numerous precedents served as a kind of regulatory guide within the temple.
A printed version was published by {{ill|Kokusho Kankōkai|ja|国書刊行会}}.
== References == * Nagamura Makoto, "Tōdaiji Yōroku" in *{{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典}} Vol. 10* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1989) ISBN 978-4-642-00510-4. * {{ill|Inaba Nobumichi|ja|稲葉伸道}}, "Tōdaiji Yōroku" in *{{ill|Nihonshi Daijiten|ja|日本史大事典}} Vol. 5* (Heibonsha, 1993) ISBN 978-4-642-00510-4. * *{{ill|Hachiman Usagu Gotakusenshū|ja|八幡宇佐宮御託宣集}}*, edited by Shinun, 1313. * Hiraoka Sadami, "Tōdaiji Yōroku" in *{{ill|Heian Jidai-shi Jiten|ja|平安時代史事典}}* (Kadokawa Shoten, 1994) ISBN 978-4-04-031700-7. * Kuno Shūgi, "Tōdaiji Yōroku" in *{{ill|Nihon Rekishi Daijiten|ja|日本歴史大事典}} Vol. 3* (Shogakukan, 2001) ISBN 978-4-09-523003-0.

'''Tōdaiji Yōroku''' (東大寺要録) is a temple chronicle of {{ill|Tōdai-ji|ja|東大寺}}, compiled in the late {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}. It consists of 10 volumes divided into 10 chapters.
== Overview == According to its preface, the chronicle was compiled in {{ill|Kashō|ja|嘉承}} 1 (1106) by a monk from Tōdaiji who wished to restore the declining temple. The editor's name remains unknown. Later, in {{ill|Chōshō|ja|長承}} 3 (1134), {{ill|Kangen|ja|観厳}} revised and supplemented the text, shaping it into the form known today. Originally a scroll manuscript, neither the original nor Kangen's version survives. However, portions of later handwritten copies, which incorporate Kangen's revisions and additional annotations, are preserved at {{ill|Daigo-ji|ja|醍醐寺}} (bearing a colophon dated {{ill|Ninji|ja|仁治}} 2 (1241)) and at Tōdaiji itself (with a colophon from {{ill|Bunmei|ja|文明}} 17 (1485)). Both copies are designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Properties of Japan|ja|重要文化財}}.
The chronicle comprises chapters on topics such as foundation prayers, origins, memorial services, sub-temples, rituals, sects, abbots, tax-exempt estates, subsidiary temples, and miscellaneous matters. It incorporates many excerpts from historical materials and documents, including sources that are now lost, making it an invaluable resource for understanding Tōdaiji's history from its founding through the Insei period. Additionally, its numerous precedents served as a kind of regulatory guide within the temple.
A printed version was published by {{ill|Kokusho Kankōkai|ja|国書刊行会}}.

'''Tōdaiji Yōroku''' (東大寺要録) is a temple chronicle of {{ill|Tōdai-ji|ja|東大寺}}, compiled in the late {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}. It consists of 10 volumes divided into 10 chapters.
== Overview == According to its preface, the chronicle was compiled in {{ill|Kashō|ja|嘉承}} 1 (1106) by a monk from Tōdaiji who wished to restore the declining temple. The editor's name remains unknown. Later, in {{ill|Chōshō|ja|長承}} 3 (1134), {{ill|Kangen|ja|観厳}} revised and supplemented the text, shaping it into the form known today. Originally a scroll manuscript, neither the original nor Kangen's version survives. However, portions of later handwritten copies, which incorporate Kangen's revisions and additional annotations, are preserved at {{ill|Daigo-ji|ja|醍醐寺}} (bearing a colophon dated {{ill|Ninji|ja|仁治}} 2 (1241)) and at Tōdaiji itself (with a colophon from {{ill|Bunmei|ja|文明}} 17 (1485)). Both copies are designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Properties of Japan|ja|重要文化財}}.
The chronicle comprises chapters on topics such as foundation prayers, origins, memorial services, sub-temples, rituals, sects, abbots, tax-exempt estates, subsidiary temples, and miscellaneous matters. It incorporates many excerpts from historical materials and documents, including sources that are now lost, making it an invaluable resource for understanding Tōdaiji's history from its founding through the Insei period. Additionally, its numerous precedents served as a kind of regulatory guide within the temple.
A printed version was published by {{ill|Kokusho Kankōkai|ja|国書刊行会}}.
== References == * Nagamura Makoto, "Tōdaiji Yōroku" in *{{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典}} Vol. 10* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1989) ISBN 978-4-642-00510-4. * {{ill|Inaba Nobumichi|ja|稲葉伸道}}, "Tōdaiji Yōroku" in *{{ill|Nihonshi Daijiten|ja|日本史大事典}} Vol. 5* (Heibonsha, 1993) ISBN 978-4-642-00510-4. * *{{ill|Hachiman Usagu Gotakusenshū|ja|八幡宇佐宮御託宣集}}*, edited by Shinun, 1313. * Hiraoka Sadami, "Tōdaiji Yōroku" in *{{ill|Heian Jidai-shi Jiten|ja|平安時代史事典}}* (Kadokawa Shoten, 1994) ISBN 978-4-04-031700-7. * Kuno Shūgi, "Tōdaiji Yōroku" in *{{ill|Nihon Rekishi Daijiten|ja|日本歴史大事典}} Vol. 3* (Shogakukan, 2001) ISBN 978-4-09-523003-0.

'''Tōdaiji Yōroku''' (東大寺要録) is a temple chronicle of {{ill|Tōdai-ji|ja|東大寺}}, compiled in the late {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}. It consists of 10 volumes divided into 10 chapters.
== Overview == According to its preface, the chronicle was compiled in {{ill|Kashō|ja|嘉承}} 1 (1106) by a monk from Tōdaiji who wished to restore the declining temple. The editor's name remains unknown. Later, in {{ill|Chōshō|ja|長承}} 3 (1134), {{ill|Kangen|ja|観厳}} revised and supplemented the text, shaping it into the form known today. Originally a scroll manuscript, neither the original nor Kangen's version survives. However, portions of later handwritten copies, which incorporate Kangen's revisions and additional annotations, are preserved at {{ill|Daigo-ji|ja|醍醐寺}} (bearing a colophon dated {{ill|Ninji|ja|仁治}} 2 (1241)) and at Tōdaiji itself (with a colophon from {{ill|Bunmei|ja|文明}} 17 (1485)). Both copies are designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Properties of Japan|ja|重要文化財}}.
The chronicle comprises chapters on topics such as foundation prayers, origins, memorial services, sub-temples, rituals, sects, abbots, tax-exempt estates, subsidiary temples, and miscellaneous matters. It incorporates many excerpts from historical materials and documents, including sources that are now lost, making it an invaluable resource for understanding Tōdaiji's history from its founding through the Insei period. Additionally, its numerous precedents served as a kind of regulatory guide within the temple.
A printed version was published by {{ill|Kokusho Kankōkai|ja|国書刊行会}}.

'''Tōdaiji Yōroku''' (東大寺要録) is a temple chronicle of {{ill|Tōdai-ji|ja|東大寺}}, compiled in the late {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}. It consists of 10 volumes divided into 10 chapters.
== Overview == According to its preface, the chronicle was compiled in {{ill|Kashō|ja|嘉承}} 1 (1106) by a monk from Tōdaiji who wished to restore the declining temple. The editor's name remains unknown. Later, in {{ill|Chōshō|ja|長承}} 3 (1134), {{ill|Kangen|ja|観厳}} revised and supplemented the text, shaping it into the form known today. Originally a scroll manuscript, neither the original nor Kangen's version survives. However, portions of later handwritten copies, which incorporate Kangen's revisions and additional annotations, are preserved at {{ill|Daigo-ji|ja|醍醐寺}} (bearing a colophon dated {{ill|Ninji|ja|仁治}} 2 (1241)) and at Tōdaiji itself (with a colophon from {{ill|Bunmei|ja|文明}} 17 (1485)). Both copies are designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Properties of Japan|ja|重要文化財}}.
The chronicle comprises chapters on topics such as foundation prayers, origins, memorial services, sub-temples, rituals, sects, abbots, tax-exempt estates, subsidiary temples, and miscellaneous matters. It incorporates many excerpts from historical materials and documents, including sources that are now lost, making it an invaluable resource for understanding Tōdaiji's history from its founding through the Insei period. Additionally, its numerous precedents served as a kind of regulatory guide within the temple.
A printed version was published by {{ill|Kokusho Kankōkai|ja|国書刊行会}}.
== References == * Nagamura Makoto, "Tōdaiji Yōroku" in *{{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典}} Vol. 10* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1989) ISBN 978-4-642-00510-4. * {{ill|Inaba Nobumichi|ja|稲葉伸道}}, "Tōdaiji Yōroku" in *{{ill|Nihonshi Daijiten|ja|日本史大事典}} Vol. 5* (Heibonsha, 1993) ISBN 978-4-642-00510-4. * *{{ill|Hachiman Usagu Gotakusenshū|ja|八幡宇佐宮御託宣集}}*, edited by Shinun, 1313. * Hiraoka Sadami, "Tōdaiji Yōroku" in *{{ill|Heian Jidai-shi Jiten|ja|平安時代史事典}}* (Kadokawa Shoten, 1994) ISBN 978-4-04-031700-7. * Kuno Shūgi, "Tōdaiji Yōroku" in *{{ill|Nihon Rekishi Daijiten|ja|日本歴史大事典}} Vol. 3* (Shogakukan, 2001) ISBN 978-4-09-523003-0.
=== Related Literature === * *Tōdaiji Yōroku*, edited by Tsutsui Eishun, Kokusho Kankōkai, 1971, revised edition 2003. * *Tōdaiji Zoku (Continued) Yōroku*, supervised by Tsutsui Kanshū, edited by the same research team, Kokusho Kankōkai, 2013. * *Essays on the Ancient World of Tōdaiji: Revisiting "Tōdaiji Yōroku"*, Tōdaiji *The Great Buddha Symposium*, Vol. 14, Hōzōkan, 2017. * *Tōdaiji Yōroku Vol. 1: Tōdaiji Series*, edited by Tōdaiji Historical Research Institute, Hōzōkan, 2019. * *Tōdaiji Yōroku Vol. 2: Tōdaiji Series*, edited by Tōdaiji Historical Research Institute, Hōzōkan, 2020.
== See Also == * {{ill|Tōdaiji Zoku Yōroku|ja|東大寺続要録}} * {{ill|Tōdaiji Bettō Shidai|ja|東大寺別当次第}}

'''Tōdaiji Yōroku''' (東大寺要録) is a temple chronicle of {{ill|Tōdai-ji|ja|東大寺}}, compiled in the late {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}. It consists of 10 volumes divided into 10 chapters.
== Overview == According to its preface, the chronicle was compiled in {{ill|Kashō|ja|嘉承}} 1 (1106) by a monk from Tōdaiji who wished to restore the declining temple. The editor's name remains unknown. Later, in {{ill|Chōshō|ja|長承}} 3 (1134), {{ill|Kangen|ja|観厳}} revised and supplemented the text, shaping it into the form known today. Originally a scroll manuscript, neither the original nor Kangen's version survives. However, portions of later handwritten copies, which incorporate Kangen's revisions and additional annotations, are preserved at {{ill|Daigo-ji|ja|醍醐寺}} (bearing a colophon dated {{ill|Ninji|ja|仁治}} 2 (1241)) and at Tōdaiji itself (with a colophon from {{ill|Bunmei|ja|文明}} 17 (1485)). Both copies are designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Properties of Japan|ja|重要文化財}}.
The chronicle comprises chapters on topics such as foundation prayers, origins, memorial services, sub-temples, rituals, sects, abbots, tax-exempt estates, subsidiary temples, and miscellaneous matters. It incorporates many excerpts from historical materials and documents, including sources that are now lost, making it an invaluable resource for understanding Tōdaiji's history from its founding through the Insei period. Additionally, its numerous precedents served as a kind of regulatory guide within the temple.
A printed version was published by {{ill|Kokusho Kankōkai|ja|国書刊行会}}.
== References == * Nagamura Makoto, "Tōdaiji Yōroku" in *{{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典}} Vol. 10* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1989) ISBN 978-4-642-00510-4. * {{ill|Inaba Nobumichi|ja|稲葉伸道}}, "Tōdaiji Yōroku" in *{{ill|Nihonshi Daijiten|ja|日本史大事典}} Vol. 5* (Heibonsha, 1993) ISBN 978-4-642-00510-4. * *{{ill|Hachiman Usagu Gotakusenshū|ja|八幡宇佐宮御託宣集}}*, edited by Shinun, 1313. * Hiraoka Sadami, "Tōdaiji Yōroku" in *{{ill|Heian Jidai-shi Jiten|ja|平安時代史事典}}* (Kadokawa Shoten, 1994) ISBN 978-4-04-031700-7. * Kuno Shūgi, "Tōdaiji Yōroku" in *{{ill|Nihon Rekishi Daijiten|ja|日本歴史大事典}} Vol. 3* (Shogakukan, 2001) ISBN 978-4-09-523003-0.
=== Related Literature === * *Tōdaiji Yōroku*, edited by Tsutsui Eishun, Kokusho Kankōkai, 1971, revised edition 2003. * *Tōdaiji Zoku (Continued) Yōroku*, supervised by Tsutsui Kanshū, edited by the same research team, Kokusho Kankōkai, 2013. * *Essays on the Ancient World of Tōdaiji: Revisiting "Tōdaiji Yōroku"*, Tōdaiji *The Great Buddha Symposium*, Vol. 14, Hōzōkan, 2017. * *Tōdaiji Yōroku Vol. 1: Tōdaiji Series*, edited by Tōdaiji Historical Research Institute, Hōzōkan, 2019. * *Tōdaiji Yōroku Vol. 2: Tōdaiji Series*, edited by Tōdaiji Historical Research Institute, Hōzōkan, 2020.
== See Also == * {{ill|Tōdaiji Zoku Yōroku|ja|東大寺続要録}} * {{ill|Tōdaiji Bettō Shidai|ja|東大寺別当次第}}

'''Tōdaiji Yōroku''' (東大寺要録) is a temple chronicle of {{ill|Tōdai-ji|ja|東大寺}}, compiled in the late {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}. It consists of 10 volumes divided into 10 chapters.
== Overview == According to its preface, the chronicle was compiled in {{ill|Kashō|ja|嘉承}} 1 (1106) by a monk from Tōdaiji who wished to restore the declining temple. The editor's name remains unknown. Later, in {{ill|Chōshō|ja|長承}} 3 (1134), {{ill|Kangen|ja|観厳}} revised and supplemented the text, shaping it into the form known today. Originally a scroll manuscript, neither the original nor Kangen's version survives. However, portions of later handwritten copies, which incorporate Kangen's revisions and additional annotations, are preserved at {{ill|Daigo-ji|ja|醍醐寺}} (bearing a colophon dated {{ill|Ninji|ja|仁治}} 2 (1241)) and at Tōdaiji itself (with a colophon from {{ill|Bunmei|ja|文明}} 17 (1485)). Both copies are designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Properties of Japan|ja|重要文化財}}.
The chronicle comprises chapters on topics such as foundation prayers, origins, memorial services, sub-temples, rituals, sects, abbots, tax-exempt estates, subsidiary temples, and miscellaneous matters. It incorporates many excerpts from historical materials and documents, including sources that are now lost, making it an invaluable resource for understanding Tōdaiji's history from its founding through the Insei period. Additionally, its numerous precedents served as a kind of regulatory guide within the temple.
A printed version was published by {{ill|Kokusho Kankōkai|ja|国書刊行会}}.
== References == * Nagamura Makoto, "Tōdaiji Yōroku" in *{{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典}} Vol. 10* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1989) ISBN 978-4-642-00510-4. * {{ill|Inaba Nobumichi|ja|稲葉伸道}}, "Tōdaiji Yōroku" in *{{ill|Nihonshi Daijiten|ja|日本史大事典}} Vol. 5* (Heibonsha, 1993) ISBN 978-4-642-00510-4. * *{{ill|Hachiman Usagu Gotakusenshū|ja|八幡宇佐宮御託宣集}}*, edited by Shinun, 1313. * Hiraoka Sadami, "Tōdaiji Yōroku" in *{{ill|Heian Jidai-shi Jiten|ja|平安時代史事典}}* (Kadokawa Shoten, 1994) ISBN 978-4-04-031700-7. * Kuno Shūgi, "Tōdaiji Yōroku" in *{{ill|Nihon Rekishi Daijiten|ja|日本歴史大事典}} Vol. 3* (Shogakukan, 2001) ISBN 978-4-09-523003-0.
=== Related Literature === * *Tōdaiji Yōroku*, edited by Tsutsui Eishun, Kokusho Kankōkai, 1971, revised edition 2003. * *Tōdaiji Zoku (Continued) Yōroku*, supervised by Tsutsui Kanshū, edited by the same research team, Kokusho Kankōkai, 2013. * *Essays on the Ancient World of Tōdaiji: Revisiting "Tōdaiji Yōroku"*, Tōdaiji *The Great Buddha Symposium*, Vol. 14, Hōzōkan, 2017. * *Tōdaiji Yōroku Vol. 1: Tōdaiji Series*, edited by Tōdaiji Historical Research Institute, Hōzōkan, 2019. * *Tōdaiji Yōroku Vol. 2: Tōdaiji Series*, edited by Tōdaiji Historical Research Institute, Hōzōkan, 2020.
== See Also == * {{ill|Tōdaiji Zoku Yōroku|ja|東大寺続要録}} * {{ill|Tōdaiji Bettō Shidai|ja|東大寺別当次第}}

'''Tōdaiji Yōroku''' (東大寺要録) is a temple chronicle of {{ill|Tōdai-ji|ja|東大寺}}, compiled in the late {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}. It consists of 10 volumes divided into 10 chapters.
== Overview == According to its preface, the chronicle was compiled in {{ill|Kashō|ja|嘉承}} 1 (1106) by a monk from Tōdaiji who wished to restore the declining temple. The editor's name remains unknown. Later, in {{ill|Chōshō|ja|長承}} 3 (1134), {{ill|Kangen|ja|観厳}} revised and supplemented the text, shaping it into the form known today. Originally a scroll manuscript, neither the original nor Kangen's version survives. However, portions of later handwritten copies, which incorporate Kangen's revisions and additional annotations, are preserved at {{ill|Daigo-ji|ja|醍醐寺}} (bearing a colophon dated {{ill|Ninji|ja|仁治}} 2 (1241)) and at Tōdaiji itself (with a colophon from {{ill|Bunmei|ja|文明}} 17 (1485)). Both copies are designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Properties of Japan|ja|重要文化財}}.
The chronicle comprises chapters on topics such as foundation prayers, origins, memorial services, sub-temples, rituals, sects, abbots, tax-exempt estates, subsidiary temples, and miscellaneous matters. It incorporates many excerpts from historical materials and documents, including sources that are now lost, making it an invaluable resource for understanding Tōdaiji's history from its founding through the Insei period. Additionally, its numerous precedents served as a kind of regulatory guide within the temple.
A printed version was published by {{ill|Kokusho Kankōkai|ja|国書刊行会}}.
== References == * Nagamura Makoto, "Tōdaiji Yōroku" in *{{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典}} Vol. 10* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1989) ISBN 978-4-642-00510-4. * {{ill|Inaba Nobumichi|ja|稲葉伸道}}, "Tōdaiji Yōroku" in *{{ill|Nihonshi Daijiten|ja|日本史大事典}} Vol. 5* (Heibonsha, 1993) ISBN 978-4-642-00510-4. * *{{ill|Hachiman Usagu Gotakusenshū|ja|八幡宇佐宮御託宣集}}*, edited by Shinun, 1313. * Hiraoka Sadami, "Tōdaiji Yōroku" in *{{ill|Heian Jidai-shi Jiten|ja|平安時代史事典}}* (Kadokawa Shoten, 1994) ISBN 978-4-04-031700-7. * Kuno Shūgi, "Tōdaiji Yōroku" in *{{ill|Nihon Rekishi Daijiten|ja|日本歴史大事典}} Vol. 3* (Shogakukan, 2001) ISBN 978-4-09-523003-0.
=== Related Literature === * *Tōdaiji Yōroku*, edited by Tsutsui Eishun, Kokusho Kankōkai, 1971, revised edition 2003. * *Tōdaiji Zoku (Continued) Yōroku*, supervised by Tsutsui Kanshū, edited by the same research team, Kokusho Kankōkai, 2013. * *Essays on the Ancient World of Tōdaiji: Revisiting "Tōdaiji Yōroku"*, Tōdaiji *The Great Buddha Symposium*, Vol. 14, Hōzōkan, 2017. * *Tōdaiji Yōroku Vol. 1: Tōdaiji Series*, edited by Tōdaiji Historical Research Institute, Hōzōkan, 2019. * *Tōdaiji Yōroku Vol. 2: Tōdaiji Series*, edited by Tōdaiji Historical Research Institute, Hōzōkan, 2020.
== See Also == * {{ill|Tōdaiji Zoku Yōroku|ja|東大寺続要録}} * {{ill|Tōdaiji Bettō Shidai|ja|東大寺別当次第}}

'''Tōdaiji Yōroku''' (東大寺要録) is a temple chronicle of {{ill|Tōdai-ji|ja|東大寺}}, compiled in the late {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}. It consists of 10 volumes divided into 10 chapters.
== Overview == According to its preface, the chronicle was compiled in {{ill|Kashō|ja|嘉承}} 1 (1106) by a monk from Tōdaiji who wished to restore the declining temple. The editor's name remains unknown. Later, in {{ill|Chōshō|ja|長承}} 3 (1134), {{ill|Kangen|ja|観厳}} revised and supplemented the text, shaping it into the form known today. Originally a scroll manuscript, neither the original nor Kangen's version survives. However, portions of later handwritten copies, which incorporate Kangen's revisions and additional annotations, are preserved at {{ill|Daigo-ji|ja|醍醐寺}} (bearing a colophon dated {{ill|Ninji|ja|仁治}} 2 (1241)) and at Tōdaiji itself (with a colophon from {{ill|Bunmei|ja|文明}} 17 (1485)). Both copies are designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Properties of Japan|ja|重要文化財}}.
The chronicle comprises chapters on topics such as foundation prayers, origins, memorial services, sub-temples, rituals, sects, abbots, tax-exempt estates, subsidiary temples, and miscellaneous matters. It incorporates many excerpts from historical materials and documents, including sources that are now lost, making it an invaluable resource for understanding Tōdaiji's history from its founding through the Insei period. Additionally, its numerous precedents served as a kind of regulatory guide within the temple.
A printed version was published by {{ill|Kokusho Kankōkai|ja|国書刊行会}}.

'''Tōdaiji Yōroku''' (東大寺要録) is a temple chronicle of {{ill|Tōdai-ji|ja|東大寺}}, compiled in the late {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}. It consists of 10 volumes divided into 10 chapters.
== Overview == According to its preface, the chronicle was compiled in {{ill|Kashō|ja|嘉承}} 1 (1106) by a monk from Tōdaiji who wished to restore the declining temple. The editor's name remains unknown. Later, in {{ill|Chōshō|ja|長承}} 3 (1134), {{ill|Kangen|ja|観厳}} revised and supplemented the text, shaping it into the form known today. Originally a scroll manuscript, neither the original nor Kangen's version survives. However, portions of later handwritten copies, which incorporate Kangen's revisions and additional annotations, are preserved at {{ill|Daigo-ji|ja|醍醐寺}} (bearing a colophon dated {{ill|Ninji|ja|仁治}} 2 (1241)) and at Tōdaiji itself (with a colophon from {{ill|Bunmei|ja|文明}} 17 (1485)). Both copies are designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Properties of Japan|ja|重要文化財}}.
The chronicle comprises chapters on topics such as foundation prayers, origins, memorial services, sub-temples, rituals, sects, abbots, tax-exempt estates, subsidiary temples, and miscellaneous matters. It incorporates many excerpts from historical materials and documents, including sources that are now lost, making it an invaluable resource for understanding Tōdaiji's history from its founding through the Insei period. Additionally, its numerous precedents served as a kind of regulatory guide within the temple.
A printed version was published by {{ill|Kokusho Kankōkai|ja|国書刊行会}}.
== References == * Nagamura Makoto, "Tōdaiji Yōroku" in *{{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典}} Vol. 10* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1989) ISBN 978-4-642-00510-4. * {{ill|Inaba Nobumichi|ja|稲葉伸道}}, "Tōdaiji Yōroku" in *{{ill|Nihonshi Daijiten|ja|日本史大事典}} Vol. 5* (Heibonsha, 1993) ISBN 978-4-642-00510-4. * *{{ill|Hachiman Usagu Gotakusenshū|ja|八幡宇佐宮御託宣集}}*, edited by Shinun, 1313. * Hiraoka Sadami, "Tōdaiji Yōroku" in *{{ill|Heian Jidai-shi Jiten|ja|平安時代史事典}}* (Kadokawa Shoten, 1994) ISBN 978-4-04-031700-7. * Kuno Shūgi, "Tōdaiji Yōroku" in *{{ill|Nihon Rekishi Daijiten|ja|日本歴史大事典}} Vol. 3* (Shogakukan, 2001) ISBN 978-4-09-523003-0.
=== Related Literature === * *Tōdaiji Yōroku*, edited by Tsutsui Eishun, Kokusho Kankōkai, 1971, revised edition 2003. * *Tōdaiji Zoku (Continued) Yōroku*, supervised by Tsutsui Kanshū, edited by the same research team, Kokusho Kankōkai, 2013. * *Essays on the Ancient World of Tōdaiji: Revisiting "Tōdaiji Yōroku"*, Tōdaiji *The Great Buddha Symposium*, Vol. 14, Hōzōkan, 2017. * *Tōdaiji Yōroku Vol. 1: Tōdaiji Series*, edited by Tōdaiji Historical Research Institute, Hōzōkan, 2019. * *Tōdaiji Yōroku Vol. 2: Tōdaiji Series*, edited by Tōdaiji Historical Research Institute, Hōzōkan, 2020. == See Also == * {{ill|Tōdaiji Zoku Yōroku|ja|東大寺続要録}} * {{ill|Tōdaiji Bettō Shidai|ja|東大寺別当次第}}

'''Tōdaiji Yōroku''' (東大寺要録) is a temple chronicle of {{ill|Tōdai-ji|ja|東大寺|12=draft}}, compiled in the late {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|12=draft}}. It consists of 10 volumes divided into 10 chapters.
== Overview == According to its preface, the chronicle was compiled in {{ill|Kashō|ja|嘉承|12=draft}} 1 (1106) by a monk from Tōdaiji who wished to restore the declining temple. The editor's name remains unknown. Later, in {{ill|Chōshō|ja|長承|12=draft}} 3 (1134), {{ill|Kangen|ja|観厳|12=draft}} revised and supplemented the text, shaping it into the form known today. Originally a scroll manuscript, neither the original nor Kangen's version survives. However, portions of later handwritten copies, which incorporate Kangen's revisions and additional annotations, are preserved at {{ill|Daigo-ji|ja|醍醐寺|12=draft}} (bearing a colophon dated {{ill|Ninji|ja|仁治|12=draft}} 2 (1241)) and at Tōdaiji itself (with a colophon from {{ill|Bunmei|ja|文明|12=draft}} 17 (1485)). Both copies are designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Properties of Japan|ja|重要文化財|12=draft}}.
The chronicle comprises chapters on topics such as foundation prayers, origins, memorial services, sub-temples, rituals, sects, abbots, tax-exempt estates, subsidiary temples, and miscellaneous matters. It incorporates many excerpts from historical materials and documents, including sources that are now lost, making it an invaluable resource for understanding Tōdaiji's history from its founding through the Insei period. Additionally, its numerous precedents served as a kind of regulatory guide within the temple.
A printed version was published by {{ill|Kokusho Kankōkai|ja|国書刊行会|12=draft}}.
== References == * Nagamura Makoto, "Tōdaiji Yōroku" in *{{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典|12=draft}} Vol. 10* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1989) ISBN 978-4-642-00510-4. * {{ill|Inaba Nobumichi|ja|稲葉伸道|12=draft}}, "Tōdaiji Yōroku" in *{{ill|Nihonshi Daijiten|ja|日本史大事典|12=draft}} Vol. 5* (Heibonsha, 1993) ISBN 978-4-642-00510-4. * *{{ill|Hachiman Usagu Gotakusenshū|ja|八幡宇佐宮御託宣集|12=draft}}*, edited by Shinun, 1313. * Hiraoka Sadami, "Tōdaiji Yōroku" in *{{ill|Heian Jidai-shi Jiten|ja|平安時代史事典|12=draft}}* (Kadokawa Shoten, 1994) ISBN 978-4-04-031700-7. * Kuno Shūgi, "Tōdaiji Yōroku" in *{{ill|Nihon Rekishi Daijiten|ja|日本歴史大事典|12=draft}} Vol. 3* (Shogakukan, 2001) ISBN 978-4-09-523003-0.
=== Related Literature === * *Tōdaiji Yōroku*, edited by Tsutsui Eishun, Kokusho Kankōkai, 1971, revised edition 2003. * *Tōdaiji Zoku (Continued) Yōroku*, supervised by Tsutsui Kanshū, edited by the same research team, Kokusho Kankōkai, 2013. * *Essays on the Ancient World of Tōdaiji: Revisiting "Tōdaiji Yōroku"*, Tōdaiji *The Great Buddha Symposium*, Vol. 14, Hōzōkan, 2017. * *Tōdaiji Yōroku Vol. 1: Tōdaiji Series*, edited by Tōdaiji Historical Research Institute, Hōzōkan, 2019. * *Tōdaiji Yōroku Vol. 2: Tōdaiji Series*, edited by Tōdaiji Historical Research Institute, Hōzōkan, 2020. == See Also == * {{ill|Tōdaiji Zoku Yōroku|ja|東大寺続要録|12=draft}} * {{ill|Tōdaiji Bettō Shidai|ja|東大寺別当次第|12=draft}}
<!--Tōdaiji Yōroku-->

revision.text:
'''東大寺要録'''（とうだいじようろく）は、[[平安時代]]後期に作成された[[東大寺]]の寺誌。10巻10章。
== 概要 == 序文によれば、[[嘉承]]元年（[[1106年]]）に東大寺の僧が衰微した寺の再興を願って編纂したとあるが、編者名などは不明。その後、[[長承]]3年（[[1134年]]）になって[[観厳]]が増補・再編して今日知られる形態になったという。元は巻子本。ただし、原本・観厳伝本ともに現存しておらず、観厳の伝本に更に補筆されたものの写本の一部が[[醍醐寺]]（[[仁治]]2年（[[1241年]]）奥書）や東大寺（[[文明 (日本)|文明]]17年（[[1485年]]）奥書）に残されており、いずれも国の[[重要文化財]]とされている。
本願・縁起・供養・諸院・諸会・諸宗・別当・封戸水田・末寺・雑事の各章からなり、今日では散逸したものも含めた多くの史料・文書からの引用記事を含んでおり、創建当時から院政期にかけての[[東大寺の歴史]]を知る上で貴重な史料となっている。また、数多くの[[先例]]を記しているため、東大寺内部においては一種の規範的効果を有していた。
[[国書刊行会]]から活字版が出版されている。
== 参考文献 == *永村眞「東大寺要録」（『[[国史大辞典 (昭和時代)|国史大辞典]] 10』（[[吉川弘文館]]、1989年） ISBN 978-4-642-00510-4） *[[稲葉伸道]]「東大寺要録」（『[[日本史大事典]] 5』（[[平凡社]]、1993年） ISBN 978-4-642-00510-4） *『[[八幡宇佐宮御託宣集]]』1313年、[[神吽]]編纂 *平岡定海「東大寺要録」（『[[平安時代史事典]]』（[[角川書店]]、1994年） ISBN 978-4-04-031700-7） *久野修義「東大寺要録」（『[[日本歴史大事典]] 3』（[[小学館]]、2001年） ISBN 978-4-09-523003-0）
=== 関連文献 === *『東大寺要録』筒井英俊校訂、国書刊行会、1971年、新版2003年 *『東大寺 續（続）要録』筒井寛秀監修、同研究会編・校訂、[[国書刊行会]]、2013年 *『論集 古代東大寺の世界 『東大寺要録』を読み直す』東大寺「[[ザ・グレイトブッダ・シンポジウム]] 第14号」[[法蔵館]]、2017年 *『東大寺要録 一 東大寺叢書』東大寺史研究所編、[[法藏館]]、2019年 *『東大寺要録 二 東大寺叢書』東大寺史研究所編、法藏館、2020年
== 関連項目 == *[[東大寺続要録]] *[[東大寺別当次第]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[東大寺要録]] to [[Tōdaiji Yōroku]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import
revision.text:
'''東大寺要録'''（とうだいじようろく）は、[[平安時代]]後期に作成された[[東大寺]]の寺誌。10巻10章。
== 概要 == 序文によれば、[[嘉承]]元年（[[1106年]]）に東大寺の僧が衰微した寺の再興を願って編纂したとあるが、編者名などは不明。その後、[[長承]]3年（[[1134年]]）になって[[観厳]]が増補・再編して今日知られる形態になったという。元は巻子本。ただし、原本・観厳伝本ともに現存しておらず、観厳の伝本に更に補筆されたものの写本の一部が[[醍醐寺]]（[[仁治]]2年（[[1241年]]）奥書）や東大寺（[[文明 (日本)|文明]]17年（[[1485年]]）奥書）に残されており、いずれも国の[[重要文化財]]とされている。
本願・縁起・供養・諸院・諸会・諸宗・別当・封戸水田・末寺・雑事の各章からなり、今日では散逸したものも含めた多くの史料・文書からの引用記事を含んでおり、創建当時から院政期にかけての[[東大寺の歴史]]を知る上で貴重な史料となっている。また、数多くの[[先例]]を記しているため、東大寺内部においては一種の規範的効果を有していた。
[[国書刊行会]]から活字版が出版されている。
== 参考文献 == *永村眞「東大寺要録」（『[[国史大辞典 (昭和時代)|国史大辞典]] 10』（[[吉川弘文館]]、1989年） ISBN 978-4-642-00510-4） *[[稲葉伸道]]「東大寺要録」（『[[日本史大事典]] 5』（[[平凡社]]、1993年） ISBN 978-4-642-00510-4） *『[[八幡宇佐宮御託宣集]]』1313年、[[神吽]]編纂 *平岡定海「東大寺要録」（『[[平安時代史事典]]』（[[角川書店]]、1994年） ISBN 978-4-04-031700-7） *久野修義「東大寺要録」（『[[日本歴史大事典]] 3』（[[小学館]]、2001年） ISBN 978-4-09-523003-0）
=== 関連文献 === *『東大寺要録』筒井英俊校訂、国書刊行会、1971年、新版2003年 *『東大寺 續（続）要録』筒井寛秀監修、同研究会編・校訂、[[国書刊行会]]、2013年 *『論集 古代東大寺の世界 『東大寺要録』を読み直す』東大寺「[[ザ・グレイトブッダ・シンポジウム]] 第14号」[[法蔵館]]、2017年 *『東大寺要録 一 東大寺叢書』東大寺史研究所編、[[法藏館]]、2019年 *『東大寺要録 二 東大寺叢書』東大寺史研究所編、法藏館、2020年
== 関連項目 == *[[東大寺続要録]] *[[東大寺別当次第]]

Bot: remove sole member from [[Category:Nara-period Buddhism]]
revision.text:
'''Tōdaiji Yōroku''' (東大寺要録) is a temple chronicle of {{ill|Tōdai-ji|ja|東大寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōdai-ji|qq=}}, compiled in the late {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heian period|qq=}}. It consists of 10 volumes divided into 10 chapters.
== Overview == According to its preface, the chronicle was compiled in {{ill|Kashō|ja|嘉承|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashō|qq=}} 1 (1106) by a monk from Tōdaiji who wished to restore the declining temple. The editor's name remains unknown. Later, in {{ill|Chōshō|ja|長承|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōshō|qq=}} 3 (1134), {{ill|Kangen|ja|観厳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kangen|qq=}} revised and supplemented the text, shaping it into the form known today. Originally a scroll manuscript, neither the original nor Kangen's version survives. However, portions of later handwritten copies, which incorporate Kangen's revisions and additional annotations, are preserved at {{ill|Daigo-ji|ja|醍醐寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daigo-ji|qq=}} (bearing a colophon dated {{ill|Ninji|ja|仁治|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninji|qq=}} 2 (1241)) and at Tōdaiji itself (with a colophon from {{ill|Bunmei|ja|文明|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunmei|qq=}} 17 (1485)). Both copies are designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Properties of Japan|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Cultural Properties of Japan|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Cultural Property (Japan)|qq=}}.
The chronicle comprises chapters on topics such as foundation prayers, origins, memorial services, sub-temples, rituals, sects, abbots, tax-exempt estates, subsidiary temples, and miscellaneous matters. It incorporates many excerpts from historical materials and documents, including sources that are now lost, making it an invaluable resource for understanding Tōdaiji's history from its founding through the Insei period. Additionally, its numerous precedents served as a kind of regulatory guide within the temple.
A printed version was published by {{ill|Kokusho Kankōkai|ja|国書刊行会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokusho Kankōkai|qq=}}.
== References == * Nagamura Makoto, "Tōdaiji Yōroku" in *{{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Daijiten|qq=}} Vol. 10* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1989) ISBN 978-4-642-00510-4. * {{ill|Inaba Nobumichi|ja|稲葉伸道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inaba Nobumichi|qq=}}, "Tōdaiji Yōroku" in *{{ill|Nihonshi Daijiten|ja|日本史大事典|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihonshi Daijiten|qq=}} Vol. 5* (Heibonsha, 1993) ISBN 978-4-642-00510-4. * *{{ill|Hachiman Usagu Gotakusenshū|ja|八幡宇佐宮御託宣集|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Usagu Gotakusenshū|qq=}}*, edited by Shinun, 1313. * Hiraoka Sadami, "Tōdaiji Yōroku" in *{{ill|Heian Jidai-shi Jiten|ja|平安時代史事典|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heian Jidai-shi Jiten|qq=}}* (Kadokawa Shoten, 1994) ISBN 978-4-04-031700-7. * Kuno Shūgi, "Tōdaiji Yōroku" in *{{ill|Nihon Rekishi Daijiten|ja|日本歴史大事典|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Daijiten|qq=}} Vol. 3* (Shogakukan, 2001) ISBN 978-4-09-523003-0.
=== Related Literature === * *Tōdaiji Yōroku*, edited by Tsutsui Eishun, Kokusho Kankōkai, 1971, revised edition 2003. * *Tōdaiji Zoku (Continued) Yōroku*, supervised by Tsutsui Kanshū, edited by the same research team, Kokusho Kankōkai, 2013. * *Essays on the Ancient World of Tōdaiji: Revisiting "Tōdaiji Yōroku"*, Tōdaiji *The Great Buddha Symposium*, Vol. 14, Hōzōkan, 2017. * *Tōdaiji Yōroku Vol. 1: Tōdaiji Series*, edited by Tōdaiji Historical Research Institute, Hōzōkan, 2019. * *Tōdaiji Yōroku Vol. 2: Tōdaiji Series*, edited by Tōdaiji Historical Research Institute, Hōzōkan, 2020. == See Also == * {{ill|Tōdaiji Zoku Yōroku|ja|東大寺続要録|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōdaiji Zoku Yōroku|qq=}} * {{ill|Tōdaiji Bettō Shidai|ja|東大寺別当次第|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōdaiji Bettō Shidai|qq=}}
{{translated page|ja|東大寺要録|version=93781362|comment=Imported full ja history}}

revision.text:
'''Tōdaiji Yōroku''' (東大寺要録) is a temple chronicle of [[:en:Tōdai-ji|Tōdai-ji]], compiled in the late [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]]. It consists of 10 volumes divided into 10 chapters.
== Overview == According to its preface, the chronicle was compiled in [[:en:Kajō|Kashō]] 1 (1106) by a monk from Tōdaiji who wished to restore the declining temple. The editor's name remains unknown. Later, in [[:en:Chōshō|Chōshō]] 3 (1134), {{ill|Kangen|ja|観厳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kangen|qq=}} revised and supplemented the text, shaping it into the form known today. Originally a scroll manuscript, neither the original nor Kangen's version survives. However, portions of later handwritten copies, which incorporate Kangen's revisions and additional annotations, are preserved at [[:en:Daigo-ji|Daigo-ji]] (bearing a colophon dated [[:en:Ninji|Ninji]] 2 (1241)) and at Tōdaiji itself (with a colophon from [[:en:Civilization|Bunmei]] 17 (1485)). Both copies are designated as [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]].
The chronicle comprises chapters on topics such as foundation prayers, origins, memorial services, sub-temples, rituals, sects, abbots, tax-exempt estates, subsidiary temples, and miscellaneous matters. It incorporates many excerpts from historical materials and documents, including sources that are now lost, making it an invaluable resource for understanding Tōdaiji's history from its founding through the Insei period. Additionally, its numerous precedents served as a kind of regulatory guide within the temple.
A printed version was published by {{ill|Kokusho Kankōkai|ja|国書刊行会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokusho Kankōkai|qq=}}.
== References == * Nagamura Makoto, "Tōdaiji Yōroku" in *{{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Daijiten|qq=}} Vol. 10* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1989) ISBN 978-4-642-00510-4. * {{ill|Inaba Nobumichi|ja|稲葉伸道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inaba Nobumichi|qq=}}, "Tōdaiji Yōroku" in *{{ill|Nihonshi Daijiten|ja|日本史大事典|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihonshi Daijiten|qq=}} Vol. 5* (Heibonsha, 1993) ISBN 978-4-642-00510-4. * *{{ill|Hachiman Usagu Gotakusenshū|ja|八幡宇佐宮御託宣集|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Usagu Gotakusenshū|qq=}}*, edited by Shinun, 1313. * Hiraoka Sadami, "Tōdaiji Yōroku" in *{{ill|Heian Jidai-shi Jiten|ja|平安時代史事典|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heian Jidai-shi Jiten|qq=}}* (Kadokawa Shoten, 1994) ISBN 978-4-04-031700-7. * Kuno Shūgi, "Tōdaiji Yōroku" in *{{ill|Nihon Rekishi Daijiten|ja|日本歴史大事典|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Daijiten|qq=}} Vol. 3* (Shogakukan, 2001) ISBN 978-4-09-523003-0.
=== Related Literature === * *Tōdaiji Yōroku*, edited by Tsutsui Eishun, Kokusho Kankōkai, 1971, revised edition 2003. * *Tōdaiji Zoku (Continued) Yōroku*, supervised by Tsutsui Kanshū, edited by the same research team, Kokusho Kankōkai, 2013. * *Essays on the Ancient World of Tōdaiji: Revisiting "Tōdaiji Yōroku"*, Tōdaiji *The Great Buddha Symposium*, Vol. 14, Hōzōkan, 2017. * *Tōdaiji Yōroku Vol. 1: Tōdaiji Series*, edited by Tōdaiji Historical Research Institute, Hōzōkan, 2019. * *Tōdaiji Yōroku Vol. 2: Tōdaiji Series*, edited by Tōdaiji Historical Research Institute, Hōzōkan, 2020. == See Also == * {{ill|Tōdaiji Zoku Yōroku|ja|東大寺続要録|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōdaiji Zoku Yōroku|qq=}} * {{ill|Tōdaiji Bettō Shidai|ja|東大寺別当次第|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōdaiji Bettō Shidai|qq=}}

revision.comment: Bot: add category Category:奈良市の重要文化財
revision.text:
'''Tōdaiji Yōroku''' (東大寺要録) is a temple chronicle of [[:en:Tōdai-ji|Tōdai-ji]], compiled in the late [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]]. It consists of 10 volumes divided into 10 chapters.
== Overview == According to its preface, the chronicle was compiled in [[:en:Kajō|Kashō]] 1 (1106) by a monk from Tōdaiji who wished to restore the declining temple. The editor's name remains unknown. Later, in [[:en:Chōshō|Chōshō]] 3 (1134), {{ill|Kangen|ja|観厳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kangen|qq=}} revised and supplemented the text, shaping it into the form known today. Originally a scroll manuscript, neither the original nor Kangen's version survives. However, portions of later handwritten copies, which incorporate Kangen's revisions and additional annotations, are preserved at [[:en:Daigo-ji|Daigo-ji]] (bearing a colophon dated [[:en:Ninji|Ninji]] 2 (1241)) and at Tōdaiji itself (with a colophon from [[:en:Civilization|Bunmei]] 17 (1485)). Both copies are designated as [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]].
The chronicle comprises chapters on topics such as foundation prayers, origins, memorial services, sub-temples, rituals, sects, abbots, tax-exempt estates, subsidiary temples, and miscellaneous matters. It incorporates many excerpts from historical materials and documents, including sources that are now lost, making it an invaluable resource for understanding Tōdaiji's history from its founding through the Insei period. Additionally, its numerous precedents served as a kind of regulatory guide within the temple.
A printed version was published by {{ill|Kokusho Kankōkai|ja|国書刊行会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokusho Kankōkai|qq=}}.
== References == * Nagamura Makoto, "Tōdaiji Yōroku" in *{{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Daijiten|qq=}} Vol. 10* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1989) ISBN 978-4-642-00510-4. * {{ill|Inaba Nobumichi|ja|稲葉伸道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inaba Nobumichi|qq=}}, "Tōdaiji Yōroku" in *{{ill|Nihonshi Daijiten|ja|日本史大事典|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihonshi Daijiten|qq=}} Vol. 5* (Heibonsha, 1993) ISBN 978-4-642-00510-4. * *{{ill|Hachiman Usagu Gotakusenshū|ja|八幡宇佐宮御託宣集|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Usagu Gotakusenshū|qq=}}*, edited by Shinun, 1313. * Hiraoka Sadami, "Tōdaiji Yōroku" in *{{ill|Heian Jidai-shi Jiten|ja|平安時代史事典|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heian Jidai-shi Jiten|qq=}}* (Kadokawa Shoten, 1994) ISBN 978-4-04-031700-7. * Kuno Shūgi, "Tōdaiji Yōroku" in *{{ill|Nihon Rekishi Daijiten|ja|日本歴史大事典|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Daijiten|qq=}} Vol. 3* (Shogakukan, 2001) ISBN 978-4-09-523003-0.
=== Related Literature === * *Tōdaiji Yōroku*, edited by Tsutsui Eishun, Kokusho Kankōkai, 1971, revised edition 2003. * *Tōdaiji Zoku (Continued) Yōroku*, supervised by Tsutsui Kanshū, edited by the same research team, Kokusho Kankōkai, 2013. * *Essays on the Ancient World of Tōdaiji: Revisiting "Tōdaiji Yōroku"*, Tōdaiji *The Great Buddha Symposium*, Vol. 14, Hōzōkan, 2017. * *Tōdaiji Yōroku Vol. 1: Tōdaiji Series*, edited by Tōdaiji Historical Research Institute, Hōzōkan, 2019. * *Tōdaiji Yōroku Vol. 2: Tōdaiji Series*, edited by Tōdaiji Historical Research Institute, Hōzōkan, 2020. == See Also == * {{ill|Tōdaiji Zoku Yōroku|ja|東大寺続要録|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōdaiji Zoku Yōroku|qq=}} * {{ill|Tōdaiji Bettō Shidai|ja|東大寺別当次第|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōdaiji Bettō Shidai|qq=}}

'''Tōdaiji Yōroku''' (東大寺要録) is a temple chronicle of [[:en:Tōdai-ji|Tōdai-ji]], compiled in the late [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]]. It consists of 10 volumes divided into 10 chapters.
== Overview == According to its preface, the chronicle was compiled in [[:en:Kajō|Kashō]] 1 (1106) by a monk from Tōdaiji who wished to restore the declining temple. The editor's name remains unknown. Later, in [[:en:Chōshō|Chōshō]] 3 (1134), {{ill|Kangen|ja|観厳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kangen|qq=}} revised and supplemented the text, shaping it into the form known today. Originally a scroll manuscript, neither the original nor Kangen's version survives. However, portions of later handwritten copies, which incorporate Kangen's revisions and additional annotations, are preserved at [[:en:Daigo-ji|Daigo-ji]] (bearing a colophon dated [[:en:Ninji|Ninji]] 2 (1241)) and at Tōdaiji itself (with a colophon from [[:en:Civilization|Bunmei]] 17 (1485)). Both copies are designated as [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]].
The chronicle comprises chapters on topics such as foundation prayers, origins, memorial services, sub-temples, rituals, sects, abbots, tax-exempt estates, subsidiary temples, and miscellaneous matters. It incorporates many excerpts from historical materials and documents, including sources that are now lost, making it an invaluable resource for understanding Tōdaiji's history from its founding through the Insei period. Additionally, its numerous precedents served as a kind of regulatory guide within the temple.
A printed version was published by {{ill|Kokusho Kankōkai|ja|国書刊行会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokusho Kankōkai|qq=}}.
== References == * Nagamura Makoto, "Tōdaiji Yōroku" in *{{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Daijiten|qq=}} Vol. 10* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1989) ISBN 978-4-642-00510-4. * {{ill|Inaba Nobumichi|ja|稲葉伸道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inaba Nobumichi|qq=}}, "Tōdaiji Yōroku" in *{{ill|Nihonshi Daijiten|ja|日本史大事典|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihonshi Daijiten|qq=}} Vol. 5* (Heibonsha, 1993) ISBN 978-4-642-00510-4. * *{{ill|Hachiman Usagu Gotakusenshū|ja|八幡宇佐宮御託宣集|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Usagu Gotakusenshū|qq=}}*, edited by Shinun, 1313. * Hiraoka Sadami, "Tōdaiji Yōroku" in *{{ill|Heian Jidai-shi Jiten|ja|平安時代史事典|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heian Jidai-shi Jiten|qq=}}* (Kadokawa Shoten, 1994) ISBN 978-4-04-031700-7. * Kuno Shūgi, "Tōdaiji Yōroku" in *{{ill|Nihon Rekishi Daijiten|ja|日本歴史大事典|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Daijiten|qq=}} Vol. 3* (Shogakukan, 2001) ISBN 978-4-09-523003-0.
=== Related Literature === * *Tōdaiji Yōroku*, edited by Tsutsui Eishun, Kokusho Kankōkai, 1971, revised edition 2003. * *Tōdaiji Zoku (Continued) Yōroku*, supervised by Tsutsui Kanshū, edited by the same research team, Kokusho Kankōkai, 2013. * *Essays on the Ancient World of Tōdaiji: Revisiting "Tōdaiji Yōroku"*, Tōdaiji *The Great Buddha Symposium*, Vol. 14, Hōzōkan, 2017. * *Tōdaiji Yōroku Vol. 1: Tōdaiji Series*, edited by Tōdaiji Historical Research Institute, Hōzōkan, 2019. * *Tōdaiji Yōroku Vol. 2: Tōdaiji Series*, edited by Tōdaiji Historical Research Institute, Hōzōkan, 2020. == See Also == * {{ill|Tōdaiji Zoku Yōroku|ja|東大寺続要録|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōdaiji Zoku Yōroku|qq=}} * {{ill|Tōdaiji Bettō Shidai|ja|東大寺別当次第|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōdaiji Bettō Shidai|qq=}}

However, portions of later handwritten copies, which incorporate Kangen's revisions and additional annotations, are preserved at [[:en:Daigo-ji|Daigo-ji]] (bearing a colophon dated [[:en:Ninji|Ninji]] 2 (1241)) and at Tōdaiji itself (with a colophon from [[:en:Civilization|Bunmei]] 17 (1485)). Both copies are designated as [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]].
The chronicle comprises chapters on topics such as foundation prayers, origins, memorial services, sub-temples, rituals, sects, abbots, tax-exempt estates, subsidiary temples, and miscellaneous matters. It incorporates many excerpts from historical materials and documents, including sources that are now lost, making it an invaluable resource for understanding Tōdaiji's history from its founding through the Insei period. Additionally, its numerous precedents served as a kind of regulatory guide within the temple.
A printed version was published by {{ill|Kokusho Kankōkai|ja|国書刊行会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokusho Kankōkai|qq=}}.

'''Tōdaiji Yōroku''' (東大寺要録) is a temple chronicle of [[:en:Tōdai-ji|Tōdai-ji]], compiled in the late [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]]. It consists of 10 volumes divided into 10 chapters.
== Overview == According to its preface, the chronicle was compiled in [[:en:Kajō|Kashō]] 1 (1106) by a monk from Tōdaiji who wished to restore the declining temple. The editor's name remains unknown. Later, in [[:en:Chōshō|Chōshō]] 3 (1134), {{ill|Kangen|ja|観厳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kangen|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} revised and supplemented the text, shaping it into the form known today. Originally a scroll manuscript, neither the original nor Kangen's version survives. However, portions of later handwritten copies, which incorporate Kangen's revisions and additional annotations, are preserved at [[:en:Daigo-ji|Daigo-ji]] (bearing a colophon dated [[:en:Ninji|Ninji]] 2 (1241)) and at Tōdaiji itself (with a colophon from [[:en:Civilization|Bunmei]] 17 (1485)). Both copies are designated as [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]].
The chronicle comprises chapters on topics such as foundation prayers, origins, memorial services, sub-temples, rituals, sects, abbots, tax-exempt estates, subsidiary temples, and miscellaneous matters. It incorporates many excerpts from historical materials and documents, including sources that are now lost, making it an invaluable resource for understanding Tōdaiji's history from its founding through the Insei period. Additionally, its numerous precedents served as a kind of regulatory guide within the temple.
A printed version was published by {{ill|Kokusho Kankōkai|ja|国書刊行会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokusho Kankōkai|qq=}}.

Later, in [[:en:Chōshō|Chōshō]] 3 (1134), {{ill|Kangen|ja|観厳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kangen|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} revised and supplemented the text, shaping it into the form known today. Originally a scroll manuscript, neither the original nor Kangen's version survives. However, portions of later handwritten copies, which incorporate Kangen's revisions and additional annotations, are preserved at [[:en:Daigo-ji|Daigo-ji]] (bearing a colophon dated [[:en:Ninji|Ninji]] 2 (1241)) and at Tōdaiji itself (with a colophon from [[:en:Civilization|Bunmei]] 17 (1485)). Both copies are designated as [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]].
The chronicle comprises chapters on topics such as foundation prayers, origins, memorial services, sub-temples, rituals, sects, abbots, tax-exempt estates, subsidiary temples, and miscellaneous matters. It incorporates many excerpts from historical materials and documents, including sources that are now lost, making it an invaluable resource for understanding Tōdaiji's history from its founding through the Insei period. Additionally, its numerous precedents served as a kind of regulatory guide within the temple.
A printed version was published by {{ill|Kokusho Kankōkai|ja|国書刊行会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokusho Kankōkai|qq=}}.
== References == * Nagamura Makoto, "Tōdaiji Yōroku" in *{{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Daijiten|qq=}} Vol. 10* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1989) ISBN 978-4-642-00510-4. * {{ill|Inaba Nobumichi|ja|稲葉伸道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inaba Nobumichi|qq=}}, "Tōdaiji Yōroku" in *{{ill|Nihonshi Daijiten|ja|日本史大事典|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihonshi Daijiten|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Vol. 5* (Heibonsha, 1993) ISBN 978-4-642-00510-4. * *{{ill|Hachiman Usagu Gotakusenshū|ja|八幡宇佐宮御託宣集|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Usagu Gotakusenshū|qq=}}*, edited by Shinun, 1313. * Hiraoka Sadami, "Tōdaiji Yōroku" in *{{ill|Heian Jidai-shi Jiten|ja|平安時代史事典|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heian Jidai-shi Jiten|qq=}}* (Kadokawa Shoten, 1994) ISBN 978-4-04-031700-7. * Kuno Shūgi, "Tōdaiji Yōroku" in *{{ill|Nihon Rekishi Daijiten|ja|日本歴史大事典|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Daijiten|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Vol. 3* (Shogakukan, 2001) ISBN 978-4-09-523003-0.

'''Tōdaiji Yōroku''' (東大寺要録) is a temple chronicle of [[:en:Tōdai-ji|Tōdai-ji]], compiled in the late [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]]. It consists of 10 volumes divided into 10 chapters.
== Overview == According to its preface, the chronicle was compiled in [[:en:Kajō|Kashō]] 1 (1106) by a monk from Tōdaiji who wished to restore the declining temple. The editor's name remains unknown. Later, in [[:en:Chōshō|Chōshō]] 3 (1134), {{ill|Kangen|ja|観厳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kangen|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} revised and supplemented the text, shaping it into the form known today. Originally a scroll manuscript, neither the original nor Kangen's version survives. However, portions of later handwritten copies, which incorporate Kangen's revisions and additional annotations, are preserved at [[:en:Daigo-ji|Daigo-ji]] (bearing a colophon dated [[:en:Ninji|Ninji]] 2 (1241)) and at Tōdaiji itself (with a colophon from [[:en:Civilization|Bunmei]] 17 (1485)). Both copies are designated as [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]].
The chronicle comprises chapters on topics such as foundation prayers, origins, memorial services, sub-temples, rituals, sects, abbots, tax-exempt estates, subsidiary temples, and miscellaneous matters. It incorporates many excerpts from historical materials and documents, including sources that are now lost, making it an invaluable resource for understanding Tōdaiji's history from its founding through the Insei period. Additionally, its numerous precedents served as a kind of regulatory guide within the temple.
A printed version was published by {{ill|Kokusho Kankōkai|ja|国書刊行会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokusho Kankōkai|qq=}}.
== References == * Nagamura Makoto, "Tōdaiji Yōroku" in *{{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Daijiten|qq=}} Vol. 10* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1989) ISBN 978-4-642-00510-4. * {{ill|Inaba Nobumichi|ja|稲葉伸道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inaba Nobumichi|qq=}}, "Tōdaiji Yōroku" in *{{ill|Nihonshi Daijiten|ja|日本史大事典|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihonshi Daijiten|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Vol. 5* (Heibonsha, 1993) ISBN 978-4-642-00510-4. * *{{ill|Hachiman Usagu Gotakusenshū|ja|八幡宇佐宮御託宣集|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Usagu Gotakusenshū|qq=}}*, edited by Shinun, 1313. * Hiraoka Sadami, "Tōdaiji Yōroku" in *{{ill|Heian Jidai-shi Jiten|ja|平安時代史事典|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heian Jidai-shi Jiten|qq=}}* (Kadokawa Shoten, 1994) ISBN 978-4-04-031700-7. * Kuno Shūgi, "Tōdaiji Yōroku" in *{{ill|Nihon Rekishi Daijiten|ja|日本歴史大事典|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Daijiten|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Vol. 3* (Shogakukan, 2001) ISBN 978-4-09-523003-0.
=== Related Literature === * *Tōdaiji Yōroku*, edited by Tsutsui Eishun, Kokusho Kankōkai, 1971, revised edition 2003. * *Tōdaiji Zoku (Continued) Yōroku*, supervised by Tsutsui Kanshū, edited by the same research team, Kokusho Kankōkai, 2013. * *Essays on the Ancient World of Tōdaiji: Revisiting "Tōdaiji Yōroku"*, Tōdaiji *The Great Buddha Symposium*, Vol. 14, Hōzōkan, 2017. * *Tōdaiji Yōroku Vol. 1: Tōdaiji Series*, edited by Tōdaiji Historical Research Institute, Hōzōkan, 2019. * *Tōdaiji Yōroku Vol. 2: Tōdaiji Series*, edited by Tōdaiji Historical Research Institute, Hōzōkan, 2020. == See Also == * {{ill|Tōdaiji Zoku Yōroku|ja|東大寺続要録|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōdaiji Zoku Yōroku|qq=}} * {{ill|Tōdaiji Bettō Shidai|ja|東大寺別当次第|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōdaiji Bettō Shidai|qq=}}

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11526470]] Wikidata name: Tōdaiji Yōroku [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:11, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Tōdaiji Yōroku''' (東大寺要録) is a temple chronicle of [[:en:Tōdai-ji|Tōdai-ji]], compiled in the late [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]]. It consists of 10 volumes divided into 10 chapters.
== Overview == According to its preface, the chronicle was compiled in [[:en:Kajō|Kashō]] 1 (1106) by a monk from Tōdaiji who wished to restore the declining temple. The editor's name remains unknown. Later, in [[:en:Chōshō|Chōshō]] 3 (1134), {{ill|Kangen|ja|観厳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kangen|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} revised and supplemented the text, shaping it into the form known today. Originally a scroll manuscript, neither the original nor Kangen's version survives. However, portions of later handwritten copies, which incorporate Kangen's revisions and additional annotations, are preserved at [[:en:Daigo-ji|Daigo-ji]] (bearing a colophon dated [[:en:Ninji|Ninji]] 2 (1241)) and at Tōdaiji itself (with a colophon from [[:en:Civilization|Bunmei]] 17 (1485)). Both copies are designated as [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]].
The chronicle comprises chapters on topics such as foundation prayers, origins, memorial services, sub-temples, rituals, sects, abbots, tax-exempt estates, subsidiary temples, and miscellaneous matters. It incorporates many excerpts from historical materials and documents, including sources that are now lost, making it an invaluable resource for understanding Tōdaiji's history from its founding through the Insei period. Additionally, its numerous precedents served as a kind of regulatory guide within the temple.
A printed version was published by {{ill|Kokusho Kankōkai|ja|国書刊行会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokusho Kankōkai|qq=}}.
== References == * Nagamura Makoto, "Tōdaiji Yōroku" in *{{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Daijiten|qq=}} Vol. 10* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1989) ISBN 978-4-642-00510-4. * {{ill|Inaba Nobumichi|ja|稲葉伸道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inaba Nobumichi|qq=}}, "Tōdaiji Yōroku" in *{{ill|Nihonshi Daijiten|ja|日本史大事典|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihonshi Daijiten|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Vol. 5* (Heibonsha, 1993) ISBN 978-4-642-00510-4. * *{{ill|Hachiman Usagu Gotakusenshū|ja|八幡宇佐宮御託宣集|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Usagu Gotakusenshū|qq=}}*, edited by Shinun, 1313. * Hiraoka Sadami, "Tōdaiji Yōroku" in *{{ill|Heian Jidai-shi Jiten|ja|平安時代史事典|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heian Jidai-shi Jiten|qq=}}* (Kadokawa Shoten, 1994) ISBN 978-4-04-031700-7. * Kuno Shūgi, "Tōdaiji Yōroku" in *{{ill|Nihon Rekishi Daijiten|ja|日本歴史大事典|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Daijiten|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Vol. 3* (Shogakukan, 2001) ISBN 978-4-09-523003-0.
=== Related Literature === * *Tōdaiji Yōroku*, edited by Tsutsui Eishun, Kokusho Kankōkai, 1971, revised edition 2003. * *Tōdaiji Zoku (Continued) Yōroku*, supervised by Tsutsui Kanshū, edited by the same research team, Kokusho Kankōkai, 2013. * *Essays on the Ancient World of Tōdaiji: Revisiting "Tōdaiji Yōroku"*, Tōdaiji *The Great Buddha Symposium*, Vol. 14, Hōzōkan, 2017. * *Tōdaiji Yōroku Vol. 1: Tōdaiji Series*, edited by Tōdaiji Historical Research Institute, Hōzōkan, 2019. * *Tōdaiji Yōroku Vol. 2: Tōdaiji Series*, edited by Tōdaiji Historical Research Institute, Hōzōkan, 2020. == See Also == * {{ill|Tōdaiji Zoku Yōroku|ja|東大寺続要録|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōdaiji Zoku Yōroku|qq=}} * {{ill|Tōdaiji Bettō Shidai|ja|東大寺別当次第|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōdaiji Bettō Shidai|qq=}}
{{translated page|ja|東大寺要録|version=93781362|comment=Imported full ja history}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:東大寺要録]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11526470]] Wikidata name: Tōdaiji Yōroku [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:11, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Tōdaiji Yōroku''' (東大寺要録) is a temple chronicle of [[:en:Tōdai-ji|Tōdai-ji]], compiled in the late [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]]. It consists of 10 volumes divided into 10 chapters.
== Overview == According to its preface, the chronicle was compiled in [[:en:Kajō|Kashō]] 1 (1106) by a monk from Tōdaiji who wished to restore the declining temple. The editor's name remains unknown. Later, in [[:en:Chōshō|Chōshō]] 3 (1134), {{ill|Kangen|ja|観厳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kangen|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} revised and supplemented the text, shaping it into the form known today. Originally a scroll manuscript, neither the original nor Kangen's version survives. However, portions of later handwritten copies, which incorporate Kangen's revisions and additional annotations, are preserved at [[:en:Daigo-ji|Daigo-ji]] (bearing a colophon dated [[:en:Ninji|Ninji]] 2 (1241)) and at Tōdaiji itself (with a colophon from [[:en:Civilization|Bunmei]] 17 (1485)). Both copies are designated as [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]].
The chronicle comprises chapters on topics such as foundation prayers, origins, memorial services, sub-temples, rituals, sects, abbots, tax-exempt estates, subsidiary temples, and miscellaneous matters. It incorporates many excerpts from historical materials and documents, including sources that are now lost, making it an invaluable resource for understanding Tōdaiji's history from its founding through the Insei period. Additionally, its numerous precedents served as a kind of regulatory guide within the temple.
A printed version was published by {{ill|Kokusho Kankōkai|ja|国書刊行会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokusho Kankōkai|qq=|1=Kokusho Kankōkai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11420946}}.
== References == * Nagamura Makoto, "Tōdaiji Yōroku" in *{{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Daijiten|qq=|1=Kokushi Daijiten|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6426904}} Vol. 10* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1989) ISBN 978-4-642-00510-4. * {{ill|Inaba Nobumichi|ja|稲葉伸道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inaba Nobumichi|qq=|1=Nobumichi Inaba|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11596905}}, "Tōdaiji Yōroku" in *{{ill|Nihonshi Daijiten|ja|日本史大事典|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihonshi Daijiten|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Vol. 5* (Heibonsha, 1993) ISBN 978-4-642-00510-4. * *{{ill|Hachiman Usagu Gotakusenshū|ja|八幡宇佐宮御託宣集|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Usagu Gotakusenshū|qq=}}*, edited by Shinun, 1313. * Hiraoka Sadami, "Tōdaiji Yōroku" in *{{ill|Heian Jidai-shi Jiten|ja|平安時代史事典|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heian Jidai-shi Jiten|qq=|1=Heian Jidaishi Jiten|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11482399}}* (Kadokawa Shoten, 1994) ISBN 978-4-04-031700-7. * Kuno Shūgi, "Tōdaiji Yōroku" in *{{ill|Nihon Rekishi Daijiten|ja|日本歴史大事典|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Daijiten|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135524488}} Vol. 3* (Shogakukan, 2001) ISBN 978-4-09-523003-0.
=== Related Literature === * *Tōdaiji Yōroku*, edited by Tsutsui Eishun, Kokusho Kankōkai, 1971, revised edition 2003. * *Tōdaiji Zoku (Continued) Yōroku*, supervised by Tsutsui Kanshū, edited by the same research team, Kokusho Kankōkai, 2013. * *Essays on the Ancient World of Tōdaiji: Revisiting "Tōdaiji Yōroku"*, Tōdaiji *The Great Buddha Symposium*, Vol. 14, Hōzōkan, 2017. * *Tōdaiji Yōroku Vol. 1: Tōdaiji Series*, edited by Tōdaiji Historical Research Institute, Hōzōkan, 2019. * *Tōdaiji Yōroku Vol. 2: Tōdaiji Series*, edited by Tōdaiji Historical Research Institute, Hōzōkan, 2020. == See Also == * {{ill|Tōdaiji Zoku Yōroku|ja|東大寺続要録|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōdaiji Zoku Yōroku|qq=|1=Tōdaiji Zoku Yōroku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11526471}} * {{ill|Tōdaiji Bettō Shidai|ja|東大寺別当次第|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōdaiji Bettō Shidai|qq=|1=Tōdaiji Bettō Shidai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11526466}}

== References == * Nagamura Makoto, "Tōdaiji Yōroku" in *{{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Daijiten|1=Kokushi Daijiten|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6426904}} Vol. 10* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1989) ISBN 978-4-642-00510-4. * {{ill|Inaba Nobumichi|ja|稲葉伸道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inaba Nobumichi|1=Nobumichi Inaba|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11596905}}, "Tōdaiji Yōroku" in *{{ill|Nihonshi Daijiten|ja|日本史大事典|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihonshi Daijiten|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Vol. 5* (Heibonsha, 1993) ISBN 978-4-642-00510-4. * *{{ill|Hachiman Usagu Gotakusenshū|ja|八幡宇佐宮御託宣集|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Usagu Gotakusenshū|qq=}}*, edited by Shinun, 1313. * Hiraoka Sadami, "Tōdaiji Yōroku" in *{{ill|Heian Jidai-shi Jiten|ja|平安時代史事典|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heian Jidai-shi Jiten|1=Heian Jidaishi Jiten|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11482399}}* (Kadokawa Shoten, 1994) ISBN 978-4-04-031700-7. * Kuno Shūgi, "Tōdaiji Yōroku" in *{{ill|Nihon Rekishi Daijiten|ja|日本歴史大事典|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Daijiten|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135524488}} Vol. 3* (Shogakukan, 2001) ISBN 978-4-09-523003-0.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:東大寺要録]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11526470]] Wikidata name: Tōdaiji Yōroku [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:11, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Tōdaiji Yōroku''' (東大寺要録) is a temple chronicle of [[:en:Tōdai-ji|Tōdai-ji]], compiled in the late [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]]. It consists of 10 volumes divided into 10 chapters.
== Overview == According to its preface, the chronicle was compiled in [[:en:Kajō|Kashō]] 1 (1106) by a monk from Tōdaiji who wished to restore the declining temple. The editor's name remains unknown. Later, in [[:en:Chōshō|Chōshō]] 3 (1134), {{ill|Kangen|ja|観厳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kangen|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} revised and supplemented the text, shaping it into the form known today. Originally a scroll manuscript, neither the original nor Kangen's version survives. However, portions of later handwritten copies, which incorporate Kangen's revisions and additional annotations, are preserved at [[:en:Daigo-ji|Daigo-ji]] (bearing a colophon dated [[:en:Ninji|Ninji]] 2 (1241)) and at Tōdaiji itself (with a colophon from [[:en:Civilization|Bunmei]] 17 (1485)). Both copies are designated as [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]].
The chronicle comprises chapters on topics such as foundation prayers, origins, memorial services, sub-temples, rituals, sects, abbots, tax-exempt estates, subsidiary temples, and miscellaneous matters. It incorporates many excerpts from historical materials and documents, including sources that are now lost, making it an invaluable resource for understanding Tōdaiji's history from its founding through the Insei period. Additionally, its numerous precedents served as a kind of regulatory guide within the temple.
A printed version was published by {{ill|Kokusho Kankōkai|ja|国書刊行会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokusho Kankōkai|1=Kokusho Kankōkai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11420946}}.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:東大寺要録]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11526470]] Wikidata name: Tōdaiji Yōroku [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:11, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Tōdaiji Yōroku''' (東大寺要録) is a temple chronicle of [[:en:Tōdai-ji|Tōdai-ji]], compiled in the late [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]]. It consists of 10 volumes divided into 10 chapters.
== Overview == According to its preface, the chronicle was compiled in [[:en:Kajō|Kashō]] 1 (1106) by a monk from Tōdaiji who wished to restore the declining temple. The editor's name remains unknown. Later, in [[:en:Chōshō|Chōshō]] 3 (1134), {{ill|Kangen|ja|観厳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kangen|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} revised and supplemented the text, shaping it into the form known today. Originally a scroll manuscript, neither the original nor Kangen's version survives. However, portions of later handwritten copies, which incorporate Kangen's revisions and additional annotations, are preserved at [[:en:Daigo-ji|Daigo-ji]] (bearing a colophon dated [[:en:Ninji|Ninji]] 2 (1241)) and at Tōdaiji itself (with a colophon from [[:en:Civilization|Bunmei]] 17 (1485)). Both copies are designated as [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]].
The chronicle comprises chapters on topics such as foundation prayers, origins, memorial services, sub-temples, rituals, sects, abbots, tax-exempt estates, subsidiary temples, and miscellaneous matters. It incorporates many excerpts from historical materials and documents, including sources that are now lost, making it an invaluable resource for understanding Tōdaiji's history from its founding through the Insei period. Additionally, its numerous precedents served as a kind of regulatory guide within the temple.
A printed version was published by {{ill|Kokusho Kankōkai|ja|国書刊行会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokusho Kankōkai|1=Kokusho Kankōkai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11420946}}.

=== Related Literature === * *Tōdaiji Yōroku*, edited by Tsutsui Eishun, Kokusho Kankōkai, 1971, revised edition 2003. * *Tōdaiji Zoku (Continued) Yōroku*, supervised by Tsutsui Kanshū, edited by the same research team, Kokusho Kankōkai, 2013. * *Essays on the Ancient World of Tōdaiji: Revisiting "Tōdaiji Yōroku"*, Tōdaiji *The Great Buddha Symposium*, Vol. 14, Hōzōkan, 2017. * *Tōdaiji Yōroku Vol. 1: Tōdaiji Series*, edited by Tōdaiji Historical Research Institute, Hōzōkan, 2019. * *Tōdaiji Yōroku Vol. 2: Tōdaiji Series*, edited by Tōdaiji Historical Research Institute, Hōzōkan, 2020. == See Also == * {{ill|Tōdaiji Zoku Yōroku|ja|東大寺続要録|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōdaiji Zoku Yōroku|1=Tōdaiji Zoku Yōroku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11526471}} * {{ill|Tōdaiji Bettō Shidai|ja|東大寺別当次第|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōdaiji Bettō Shidai|1=Tōdaiji Bettō Shidai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11526466}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:東大寺要録]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11526470]] Wikidata name: Tōdaiji Yōroku [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:11, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Tōdaiji Yōroku''' (東大寺要録) is a temple chronicle of [[:en:Tōdai-ji|Tōdai-ji]], compiled in the late [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]]. It consists of 10 volumes divided into 10 chapters.
== Overview == According to its preface, the chronicle was compiled in [[:en:Kajō|Kashō]] 1 (1106) by a monk from Tōdaiji who wished to restore the declining temple. The editor's name remains unknown. Later, in [[:en:Chōshō|Chōshō]] 3 (1134), {{ill|Kangen|ja|観厳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kangen|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} revised and supplemented the text, shaping it into the form known today. Originally a scroll manuscript, neither the original nor Kangen's version survives. However, portions of later handwritten copies, which incorporate Kangen's revisions and additional annotations, are preserved at [[:en:Daigo-ji|Daigo-ji]] (bearing a colophon dated [[:en:Ninji|Ninji]] 2 (1241)) and at Tōdaiji itself (with a colophon from [[:en:Civilization|Bunmei]] 17 (1485)). Both copies are designated as [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]].
The chronicle comprises chapters on topics such as foundation prayers, origins, memorial services, sub-temples, rituals, sects, abbots, tax-exempt estates, subsidiary temples, and miscellaneous matters. It incorporates many excerpts from historical materials and documents, including sources that are now lost, making it an invaluable resource for understanding Tōdaiji's history from its founding through the Insei period. Additionally, its numerous precedents served as a kind of regulatory guide within the temple.
A printed version was published by {{ill|Kokusho Kankōkai|ja|国書刊行会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokusho Kankōkai|1=Kokusho Kankōkai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11420946}}.
