<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:東大寺要録]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11526470]] Wikidata name: Tōdaiji Yōroku [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:11, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Tōdaiji Yōroku''' (東大寺要録) is a temple chronicle of [[:en:Tōdai-ji|Tōdai-ji]], compiled in the late [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]]. It consists of 10 volumes divided into 10 chapters.
== Overview == According to its preface, the chronicle was compiled in [[:en:Kajō|Kashō]] 1 (1106) by a monk from Tōdaiji who wished to restore the declining temple. The editor's name remains unknown. Later, in [[:en:Chōshō|Chōshō]] 3 (1134), {{ill|Kangen|ja|観厳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kangen|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} revised and supplemented the text, shaping it into the form known today. Originally a scroll manuscript, neither the original nor Kangen's version survives. However, portions of later handwritten copies, which incorporate Kangen's revisions and additional annotations, are preserved at [[:en:Daigo-ji|Daigo-ji]] (bearing a colophon dated [[:en:Ninji|Ninji]] 2 (1241)) and at Tōdaiji itself (with a colophon from [[:en:Civilization|Bunmei]] 17 (1485)). Both copies are designated as [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]].
The chronicle comprises chapters on topics such as foundation prayers, origins, memorial services, sub-temples, rituals, sects, abbots, tax-exempt estates, subsidiary temples, and miscellaneous matters. It incorporates many excerpts from historical materials and documents, including sources that are now lost, making it an invaluable resource for understanding Tōdaiji's history from its founding through the Insei period. Additionally, its numerous precedents served as a kind of regulatory guide within the temple.
A printed version was published by {{ill|Kokusho Kankōkai|ja|国書刊行会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokusho Kankōkai|1=Kokusho Kankōkai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11420946}}.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:東大寺要録]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:東大寺要録]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:東大寺要録]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11526470]] Wikidata name: Tōdaiji Yōroku [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:11, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Tōdaiji Yōroku''' (東大寺要録) is a temple chronicle of [[:en:Tōdai-ji|Tōdai-ji]], compiled in the late [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]]. It consists of 10 volumes divided into 10 chapters.
== Overview == According to its preface, the chronicle was compiled in [[:en:Kajō|Kashō]] 1 (1106) by a monk from Tōdaiji who wished to restore the declining temple. The editor's name remains unknown. Later, in [[:en:Chōshō|Chōshō]] 3 (1134), {{ill|Kangen|ja|観厳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kangen|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} revised and supplemented the text, shaping it into the form known today. Originally a scroll manuscript, neither the original nor Kangen's version survives. However, portions of later handwritten copies, which incorporate Kangen's revisions and additional annotations, are preserved at [[:en:Daigo-ji|Daigo-ji]] (bearing a colophon dated [[:en:Ninji|Ninji]] 2 (1241)) and at Tōdaiji itself (with a colophon from [[:en:Civilization|Bunmei]] 17 (1485)). Both copies are designated as [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]].
The chronicle comprises chapters on topics such as foundation prayers, origins, memorial services, sub-temples, rituals, sects, abbots, tax-exempt estates, subsidiary temples, and miscellaneous matters. It incorporates many excerpts from historical materials and documents, including sources that are now lost, making it an invaluable resource for understanding Tōdaiji's history from its founding through the Insei period. Additionally, its numerous precedents served as a kind of regulatory guide within the temple.
A printed version was published by {{ill|Kokusho Kankōkai|ja|国書刊行会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokusho Kankōkai|1=Kokusho Kankōkai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11420946}}.

=== Related Literature === * *Tōdaiji Yōroku*, edited by Tsutsui Eishun, Kokusho Kankōkai, 1971, revised edition 2003. * *Tōdaiji Zoku (Continued) Yōroku*, supervised by Tsutsui Kanshū, edited by the same research team, Kokusho Kankōkai, 2013. * *Essays on the Ancient World of Tōdaiji: Revisiting "Tōdaiji Yōroku"*, Tōdaiji *The Great Buddha Symposium*, Vol. 14, Hōzōkan, 2017. * *Tōdaiji Yōroku Vol. 1: Tōdaiji Series*, edited by Tōdaiji Historical Research Institute, Hōzōkan, 2019. * *Tōdaiji Yōroku Vol. 2: Tōdaiji Series*, edited by Tōdaiji Historical Research Institute, Hōzōkan, 2020. == See Also == * {{ill|Tōdaiji Zoku Yōroku|ja|東大寺続要録|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōdaiji Zoku Yōroku|1=Tōdaiji Zoku Yōroku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11526471}} * {{ill|Tōdaiji Bettō Shidai|ja|東大寺別当次第|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōdaiji Bettō Shidai|1=Tōdaiji Bettō Shidai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11526466}}

{{wikidata link|Q11526470}} [[Category:12th-century books]] [[Category:Buddhism in the Heian period]] [[Category:Buddhism in the Nara period]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Cultural Properties of Todai-ji]] [[Category:History books about Buddhism]] [[Category:History books of the Heian Period]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties in Nara City]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:東大寺要録]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:東大寺要録]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:東大寺要録]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11526470]] Wikidata name: Tōdaiji Yōroku [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:11, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Tōdaiji Yōroku''' (東大寺要録) is a temple chronicle of [[:en:Tōdai-ji|Tōdai-ji]], compiled in the late [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]]. It consists of 10 volumes divided into 10 chapters.
== Overview == According to its preface, the chronicle was compiled in [[:en:Kajō|Kashō]] 1 (1106) by a monk from Tōdaiji who wished to restore the declining temple. The editor's name remains unknown. Later, in [[:en:Chōshō|Chōshō]] 3 (1134), {{ill|Kangen|ja|観厳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kangen|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} revised and supplemented the text, shaping it into the form known today. Originally a scroll manuscript, neither the original nor Kangen's version survives. However, portions of later handwritten copies, which incorporate Kangen's revisions and additional annotations, are preserved at [[:en:Daigo-ji|Daigo-ji]] (bearing a colophon dated [[:en:Ninji|Ninji]] 2 (1241)) and at Tōdaiji itself (with a colophon from [[:en:Civilization|Bunmei]] 17 (1485)). Both copies are designated as [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]].
The chronicle comprises chapters on topics such as foundation prayers, origins, memorial services, sub-temples, rituals, sects, abbots, tax-exempt estates, subsidiary temples, and miscellaneous matters. It incorporates many excerpts from historical materials and documents, including sources that are now lost, making it an invaluable resource for understanding Tōdaiji's history from its founding through the Insei period. Additionally, its numerous precedents served as a kind of regulatory guide within the temple.
A printed version was published by {{ill|Kokusho Kankōkai|ja|国書刊行会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokusho Kankōkai|1=Kokusho Kankōkai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11420946}}.
== References == * Nagamura Makoto, "Tōdaiji Yōroku" in *{{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Daijiten|1=Kokushi Daijiten|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6426904}} Vol. 10* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1989) ISBN 978-4-642-00510-4. * {{ill|Inaba Nobumichi|ja|稲葉伸道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inaba Nobumichi|1=Nobumichi Inaba|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11596905}}, "Tōdaiji Yōroku" in *{{ill|Nihonshi Daijiten|ja|日本史大事典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihonshi Daijiten|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Vol. 5* (Heibonsha, 1993) ISBN 978-4-642-00510-4. * *{{ill|Hachiman Usagu Gotakusenshū|ja|八幡宇佐宮御託宣集|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Usagu Gotakusenshū|qq=}}*, edited by Shinun, 1313. * Hiraoka Sadami, "Tōdaiji Yōroku" in *{{ill|Heian Jidai-shi Jiten|ja|平安時代史事典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heian Jidai-shi Jiten|1=Heian Jidaishi Jiten|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11482399}}* (Kadokawa Shoten, 1994) ISBN 978-4-04-031700-7. * Kuno Shūgi, "Tōdaiji Yōroku" in *{{ill|Nihon Rekishi Daijiten|ja|日本歴史大事典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Daijiten|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135524488}} Vol. 3* (Shogakukan, 2001) ISBN 978-4-09-523003-0.
=== Related Literature === * *Tōdaiji Yōroku*, edited by Tsutsui Eishun, Kokusho Kankōkai, 1971, revised edition 2003. * *Tōdaiji Zoku (Continued) Yōroku*, supervised by Tsutsui Kanshū, edited by the same research team, Kokusho Kankōkai, 2013. * *Essays on the Ancient World of Tōdaiji: Revisiting "Tōdaiji Yōroku"*, Tōdaiji *The Great Buddha Symposium*, Vol. 14, Hōzōkan, 2017. * *Tōdaiji Yōroku Vol. 1: Tōdaiji Series*, edited by Tōdaiji Historical Research Institute, Hōzōkan, 2019. * *Tōdaiji Yōroku Vol. 2: Tōdaiji Series*, edited by Tōdaiji Historical Research Institute, Hōzōkan, 2020. == See Also == * {{ill|Tōdaiji Zoku Yōroku|ja|東大寺続要録|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōdaiji Zoku Yōroku|1=Tōdaiji Zoku Yōroku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11526471}} * {{ill|Tōdaiji Bettō Shidai|ja|東大寺別当次第|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōdaiji Bettō Shidai|1=Tōdaiji Bettō Shidai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11526466}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:東大寺要録]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:東大寺要録]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:東大寺要録]]
'''Tōdaiji Yōroku''' (東大寺要録) is a temple chronicle of {{ill|Tōdai-ji|en|Tōdai-ji|WD=Q460367|lt=Tōdai-ji}}, compiled in the late {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}}. It consists of 10 volumes divided into 10 chapters.
== Overview == According to its preface, the chronicle was compiled in {{ill|Kajō|en|Kajō|WD=Q1193892|lt=Kashō}} 1 (1106) by a monk from Tōdaiji who wished to restore the declining temple. The editor's name remains unknown. Later, in {{ill|Chōshō|en|Chōshō|WD=Q1194264|lt=Chōshō}} 3 (1134), {{ill|Kangen|ja|観厳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kangen|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} revised and supplemented the text, shaping it into the form known today. Originally a scroll manuscript, neither the original nor Kangen's version survives. However, portions of later handwritten copies, which incorporate Kangen's revisions and additional annotations, are preserved at {{ill|Daigo-ji|en|Daigo-ji|WD=Q1157535|lt=Daigo-ji}} (bearing a colophon dated {{ill|Ninji|en|Ninji|WD=Q1194262|lt=Ninji}} 2 (1241)) and at Tōdaiji itself (with a colophon from {{ill|Civilization|en|Civilization|WD=Q8432|lt=Bunmei}} 17 (1485)). Both copies are designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property}}.
The chronicle comprises chapters on topics such as foundation prayers, origins, memorial services, sub-temples, rituals, sects, abbots, tax-exempt estates, subsidiary temples, and miscellaneous matters. It incorporates many excerpts from historical materials and documents, including sources that are now lost, making it an invaluable resource for understanding Tōdaiji's history from its founding through the Insei period. Additionally, its numerous precedents served as a kind of regulatory guide within the temple.
A printed version was published by {{ill|Kokusho Kankōkai|ja|国書刊行会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokusho Kankōkai|1=Kokusho Kankōkai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11420946}}.
== References == * Nagamura Makoto, "Tōdaiji Yōroku" in *{{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Daijiten|1=Kokushi Daijiten|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6426904}} Vol. 10* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1989) ISBN 978-4-642-00510-4. * {{ill|Inaba Nobumichi|ja|稲葉伸道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inaba Nobumichi|1=Nobumichi Inaba|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11596905}}, "Tōdaiji Yōroku" in *{{ill|Nihonshi Daijiten|ja|日本史大事典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihonshi Daijiten|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Vol. 5* (Heibonsha, 1993) ISBN 978-4-642-00510-4. * *{{ill|Hachiman Usagu Gotakusenshū|ja|八幡宇佐宮御託宣集|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Usagu Gotakusenshū|qq=}}*, edited by Shinun, 1313. * Hiraoka Sadami, "Tōdaiji Yōroku" in *{{ill|Heian Jidai-shi Jiten|ja|平安時代史事典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heian Jidai-shi Jiten|1=Heian Jidaishi Jiten|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11482399}}* (Kadokawa Shoten, 1994) ISBN 978-4-04-031700-7. * Kuno Shūgi, "Tōdaiji Yōroku" in *{{ill|Nihon Rekishi Daijiten|ja|日本歴史大事典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Daijiten|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135524488}} Vol. 3* (Shogakukan, 2001) ISBN 978-4-09-523003-0.
=== Related Literature === * *Tōdaiji Yōroku*, edited by Tsutsui Eishun, Kokusho Kankōkai, 1971, revised edition 2003. * *Tōdaiji Zoku (Continued) Yōroku*, supervised by Tsutsui Kanshū, edited by the same research team, Kokusho Kankōkai, 2013. * *Essays on the Ancient World of Tōdaiji: Revisiting "Tōdaiji Yōroku"*, Tōdaiji *The Great Buddha Symposium*, Vol. 14, Hōzōkan, 2017. * *Tōdaiji Yōroku Vol. 1: Tōdaiji Series*, edited by Tōdaiji Historical Research Institute, Hōzōkan, 2019. * *Tōdaiji Yōroku Vol. 2: Tōdaiji Series*, edited by Tōdaiji Historical Research Institute, Hōzōkan, 2020. == See Also == * {{ill|Tōdaiji Zoku Yōroku|ja|東大寺続要録|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōdaiji Zoku Yōroku|1=Tōdaiji Zoku Yōroku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11526471}} * {{ill|Tōdaiji Bettō Shidai|ja|東大寺別当次第|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōdaiji Bettō Shidai|1=Tōdaiji Bettō Shidai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11526466}}

== References == * Nagamura Makoto, "Tōdaiji Yōroku" in *{{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Daijiten|1=National History Encyclopedia|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6426904}} Vol. 10* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1989) ISBN 978-4-642-00510-4. * {{ill|Inaba Nobumichi|ja|稲葉伸道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inaba Nobumichi|1=Nobumichi Inaba|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11596905}}, "Tōdaiji Yōroku" in *{{ill|Nihonshi Daijiten|ja|日本史大事典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihonshi Daijiten|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Vol. 5* (Heibonsha, 1993) ISBN 978-4-642-00510-4. * *{{ill|Hachiman Usagu Gotakusenshū|ja|八幡宇佐宮御託宣集|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Usagu Gotakusenshū|qq=}}*, edited by Shinun, 1313. * Hiraoka Sadami, "Tōdaiji Yōroku" in *{{ill|Heian Jidai-shi Jiten|ja|平安時代史事典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heian Jidai-shi Jiten|1=Heian Jidaishi Jiten|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11482399}}* (Kadokawa Shoten, 1994) ISBN 978-4-04-031700-7. * Kuno Shūgi, "Tōdaiji Yōroku" in *{{ill|Nihon Rekishi Daijiten|ja|日本歴史大事典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Daijiten|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135524488|1=Nihon Rekishi Daijiten}} Vol. 3* (Shogakukan, 2001) ISBN 978-4-09-523003-0.
=== Related Literature === * *Tōdaiji Yōroku*, edited by Tsutsui Eishun, Kokusho Kankōkai, 1971, revised edition 2003. * *Tōdaiji Zoku (Continued) Yōroku*, supervised by Tsutsui Kanshū, edited by the same research team, Kokusho Kankōkai, 2013. * *Essays on the Ancient World of Tōdaiji: Revisiting "Tōdaiji Yōroku"*, Tōdaiji *The Great Buddha Symposium*, Vol. 14, Hōzōkan, 2017. * *Tōdaiji Yōroku Vol. 1: Tōdaiji Series*, edited by Tōdaiji Historical Research Institute, Hōzōkan, 2019. * *Tōdaiji Yōroku Vol. 2: Tōdaiji Series*, edited by Tōdaiji Historical Research Institute, Hōzōkan, 2020. == See Also == * {{ill|Tōdaiji Zoku Yōroku|ja|東大寺続要録|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōdaiji Zoku Yōroku|1=Tōdaiji Zoku Yōroku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11526471}} * {{ill|Tōdaiji Bettō Shidai|ja|東大寺別当次第|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōdaiji Bettō Shidai|1=Tōdaiji Bettō Shidai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11526466}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:東大寺要録]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:東大寺要録]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:東大寺要録]]
'''Tōdaiji Yōroku''' (東大寺要録) is a temple chronicle of {{ill|Tōdai-ji|en|Tōdai-ji|WD=Q460367|lt=Tōdai-ji|1=Tōdai-ji}}, compiled in the late {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}. It consists of 10 volumes divided into 10 chapters.
== Overview == According to its preface, the chronicle was compiled in {{ill|Kajō|en|Kajō|WD=Q1193892|lt=Kashō|1=Kajō}} 1 (1106) by a monk from Tōdaiji who wished to restore the declining temple. The editor's name remains unknown. Later, in {{ill|Chōshō|en|Chōshō|WD=Q1194264|lt=Chōshō|1=Chōshō}} 3 (1134), {{ill|Kangen|ja|観厳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kangen|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} revised and supplemented the text, shaping it into the form known today. Originally a scroll manuscript, neither the original nor Kangen's version survives. However, portions of later handwritten copies, which incorporate Kangen's revisions and additional annotations, are preserved at {{ill|Daigo-ji|en|Daigo-ji|WD=Q1157535|lt=Daigo-ji|1=Daigo-ji}} (bearing a colophon dated {{ill|Ninji|en|Ninji|WD=Q1194262|lt=Ninji|1=Ninji}} 2 (1241)) and at Tōdaiji itself (with a colophon from {{ill|Civilization|en|Civilization|WD=Q8432|lt=Bunmei|1=Civilization}} 17 (1485)). Both copies are designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}.
The chronicle comprises chapters on topics such as foundation prayers, origins, memorial services, sub-temples, rituals, sects, abbots, tax-exempt estates, subsidiary temples, and miscellaneous matters. It incorporates many excerpts from historical materials and documents, including sources that are now lost, making it an invaluable resource for understanding Tōdaiji's history from its founding through the Insei period. Additionally, its numerous precedents served as a kind of regulatory guide within the temple.
A printed version was published by {{ill|Kokusho Kankōkai|ja|国書刊行会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokusho Kankōkai|1=Kokusho Kankōkai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11420946}}.
== References == * Nagamura Makoto, "Tōdaiji Yōroku" in *{{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Daijiten|1=National History Encyclopedia|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6426904}} Vol. 10* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1989) ISBN 978-4-642-00510-4. * {{ill|Inaba Nobumichi|ja|稲葉伸道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inaba Nobumichi|1=Nobumichi Inaba|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11596905}}, "Tōdaiji Yōroku" in *{{ill|Nihonshi Daijiten|ja|日本史大事典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihonshi Daijiten|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Vol. 5* (Heibonsha, 1993) ISBN 978-4-642-00510-4. * *{{ill|Hachiman Usagu Gotakusenshū|ja|八幡宇佐宮御託宣集|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Usagu Gotakusenshū|qq=}}*, edited by Shinun, 1313. * Hiraoka Sadami, "Tōdaiji Yōroku" in *{{ill|Heian Jidai-shi Jiten|ja|平安時代史事典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heian Jidai-shi Jiten|1=Heian Jidaishi Jiten|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11482399}}* (Kadokawa Shoten, 1994) ISBN 978-4-04-031700-7. * Kuno Shūgi, "Tōdaiji Yōroku" in *{{ill|Nihon Rekishi Daijiten|ja|日本歴史大事典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Daijiten|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135524488|1=Nihon Rekishi Daijiten}} Vol. 3* (Shogakukan, 2001) ISBN 978-4-09-523003-0.
=== Related Literature === * *Tōdaiji Yōroku*, edited by Tsutsui Eishun, Kokusho Kankōkai, 1971, revised edition 2003. * *Tōdaiji Zoku (Continued) Yōroku*, supervised by Tsutsui Kanshū, edited by the same research team, Kokusho Kankōkai, 2013. * *Essays on the Ancient World of Tōdaiji: Revisiting "Tōdaiji Yōroku"*, Tōdaiji *The Great Buddha Symposium*, Vol. 14, Hōzōkan, 2017. * *Tōdaiji Yōroku Vol. 1: Tōdaiji Series*, edited by Tōdaiji Historical Research Institute, Hōzōkan, 2019. * *Tōdaiji Yōroku Vol. 2: Tōdaiji Series*, edited by Tōdaiji Historical Research Institute, Hōzōkan, 2020. == See Also == * {{ill|Tōdaiji Zoku Yōroku|ja|東大寺続要録|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōdaiji Zoku Yōroku|1=Tōdaiji Zoku Yōroku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11526471}} * {{ill|Tōdaiji Bettō Shidai|ja|東大寺別当次第|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōdaiji Bettō Shidai|1=Tōdaiji Bettō Shidai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11526466}}
